programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: An aggressive female pharmaceuticals representative stalks the hospital's new chief of staff, hoping to break up his marriage and have him all to herself - and it is not long before Dr Sloan has another murder investigation on his hands.(n)

programme.title: Matlock
programme.desc: Part two of two. Matlock uncovers the truth behind the case, but it may not be enough to bring him victory.(n)

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Crying Cherub

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: An old friend turns to Walker for help when his pregnant daughter falls under the spell of a religious cult. Adventure with the modern-day ranger whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West. Starring Chuck Norris.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A patrolman is murdered during a routine traffic stop, and Horatio and the team discover the vehicle used during the crime was driven by a 16-year-old student. She denies being involved in the killing and immediately implicates someone else, but tests on the recovered murder weapon suggest she is lying. The girl's mother continues to defend her, until events take a dramatic turn.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The body of a Navy lieutenant is found on the side of a remote road and toxicology tests reveal he died of overhydration. When another man is found dead nearby, the NCIS team begins to suspect a serial killer is on the loose. Meanwhile, Ducky introduces Gibbs to his new girlfriend. Crime drama, guest starring Cheryl Ladd.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part one of two. The agents investigate when two men are killed at a gun shop, but soon discover one of their colleagues could be at risk. Callen learns the Chameleon - a criminal out for revenge - is involved, and races to track him down. However, when he comes face to face with the fugitive, he is tricked into answering a phone wired to a car bomb.(n)

programme.title: Matlock
programme.desc: A lawyer is accused of murdering his partner. Matlock takes up the case, and discovers a connection with the owner of a local water park.(n)

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Nimble Nephew
programme.desc: A large piece of land, soon due to be worth five times the going price, causes conflict between family members.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. The safe house is attacked, leaving Vance in hospital and Eli missing, with his bodyguard Amit Hadar dead. The movements of all the Palestinians are accounted for at the time of the attack, making it unclear who was responsible, and Ziva suspects her Mossad replacement Liat is hiding something. Drama, starring Michael Nouri, Cote de Pablo, Rocky Carroll and Mark Harmon.(n)

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker tackles a corrupt parole officer who refuses to allow one his charges to go straight and tries to blackmail him into committing another robbery.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: American social worker Sarah Goode is taken hostage by militants in northern Syria, and thanks to the efforts of the Navy Seals, is eventually returned home unharmed. Evidence from the incident suggests US citizen Kyle Nasry was connected to the kidnapping, but when the agents trace Nasry to a rally held by a controversial imam, they are surprised to find Sarah among the attendees. Meanwhile, DiNozzo finally takes his relationship with Zoe Keates public - surprising none of his colleagues. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Chameleon's planted clues lead the team on a wild goose chase as it searches for NSA agent Mike Atley, who the criminal claims to have in his custody. Callen is faced with a difficult decision when he is told to release the Chameleon in order to save Atley's life, and contemplates subduing his nemesis once and for all.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy officer scheduled to testify in a forthcoming murder trial goes missing after receiving a threat at the safe house where he was staying, and the deputy district attorney asks the NCIS agents to locate him. One of their few leads is a dead turkey Tony and McGee find in the man's fridge, so they take it to Ducky for an autopsy. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray and Michael Weatherly.(n)

programme.desc: Gibbs and his colleagues investigate when Eric Kutzler, the first openly gay Navy lieutenant tipped to receive the Medal of Honour, is murdered. Meanwhile, the team throws a baby shower for Palmer as his wife's due date approaches. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.desc: Agents G Callen and Sam Hanna investigate after a naval officer is killed during an LAPD shootout, with two other men who died in the gun battle being identified as members of a Mexican drug cartel. Spin-off drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects, an elite NCIS division specialising in undercover assignments. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents investigate the murder of a marine during a cruise. They discover he came from a famous and wealthy family but had turned his back on a life of privilege to join the military, and after the death of his father had inherited a large part of his business. His decision to change his will may have pushed one of his relatives to kill him to keep the firm in their control. Crime drama, guest-starring Diane Neal (Law & Order: Special Victims Unit).(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The team finds a corpse that appears to have been eaten by a large snake. programme.desc: Three men stage a raid on a drug deal, taking a suitcase full of cash and killing the dealer and an undercover LAPD officer. While studying footage taken from CCTV equipment in the house, Sam recognises the distinctive hand signals of one of the suspects as being those of Navy Seal Kurt Holgate, who he served with in Bosnia. Eric studies the details of his unit and finds that a man called Jackson has been unaccounted for since the day of the attack. Drama, starring LL Cool J.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy recruitment officer is murdered at a high school and the agents discover he had recently begun a relationship with a married teacher whose wife held a grudge against him. Crime drama, guest starring comedian Bob Newhart.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates when fellow agent Lara Macy's body is found badly burnt in a campfire on Annapolis beach. According to her laptop she was tailing an unco-operative rape victim, and the prime suspect in the crime is the son of a DC contractor. Gibbs knew Macy years ago, and he finds he can no longer avoid his Mexican past catching up with him as the case progresses. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly.(n)

programme.desc: The team tries to locate a kidnapped girl but has to contend with her grandfather, a former member of a disbanded special forces unit. The agents discover he has been secretly negotiating with the kidnapper, who was once in his undercover team and has decided to kill off his old colleagues in a bid to atone for their actions. Meanwhile, Abby is forced to work with an intern, and makes him follow her idiosyncratic rules. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called to the home of a bomb disposal expert who shot and killed an intruder during a robbery. The case appears to be straightforward until Tony discovers she lied about being alone during the incident, and the evidence suggests a link to a case in which a man walked free after paralysing a member of the public in a shootout. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team enlists a former secret service agent to help try to prevent a nuclear attack on an American ally, after an attempted hacker tries to launch a weapon of mass destruction. Meanwhile, Paige considers staying in Los Angeles or moving 3,000 miles away to Portland, Maine, so Ralph can attend an exclusive school. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team searches for stolen experimental shoulder-launch rockets, which are rumoured to being transported on a luxury cruise ship. Meanwhile, Ralph impresses his first crush with the help of Happy and Toby. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong.(n)

programme.title: The High Chaparral
programme.desc: Blue kills the son of a military officer who was attacking a girl. The Cannons try to find the girl so she can tell her side of the story before he is hanged for murder.(n)

programme.title: The High Chaparral
programme.desc: Buck finds an old friend lost in the desert and brings him home, only to find he is a wanted fugitive.(n)

programme.title: Bondi Rescue
programme.desc: The work of lifeguards in Sydney, Australia, as they help deal with jellyfish stings, investigate shark sightings and tend people with health problems. In this edition, the summer season becomes even busier thanks to a host of emergency calls that turn out to be false alarms.(n)

programme.title: Bondi Rescue
programme.desc: The lifeguards contend with a crowded beach during the Christmas period - dealing with five young Irishmen who followed a ball out to sea and became stranded. They also rescue a man found in a cave and help a child who has been separated from his parents.(n)

programme.title: Hoarders
programme.desc: Jackie has filled her home with $1million of teddy bears, while Richard reacts to his family's pleas for an emergency clean-up of his house. Series examining hoarders whose obsessions are taking over their lives.(n)

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries
programme.desc: The story of Joseph Prushinowski, the 'Robin Hood Rabbi' who gave the profits of his financial scams to charity. Plus, a dying baby who made a miraculous recovery at Christmas.(n)

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries
programme.desc: The stories of a conman who chooses his victims from personal ads and an assassination that may have claimed the wrong target.(n)

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A car is stolen and burned after a 5-year-old leaves the keys in the vehicle's door; angry car owners point the finger at a troubled neighborhood teen.(n)

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Embezzlement charges and a vandalized cab pit a taxi driver and his former boss against each other in a battle over liability.(n)

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A teenager hangs up on Judge Judy when she calls to investigate his sister's relationship with a man who claims she ripped him off.(n)

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Pain-killer prescription fraud, a conviction on 28 counts, and a jail sentence come into play when an ex-convict is sued by a former friend.(n)

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: Allison reinvestigates a 10-year-old murder case, and finds that the man convicted was innocent. Worse still, he was framed by somebody she knows. Paranormal drama, starring Patricia Arquette as a woman plagued by visions of the dead.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: A new code exploits a printer's design flaw, allowing cyber-arsonists to set fires remotely by using wi-fi data connections, and Brody gets a blast from the past when he discovers the person responsible. Meanwhile, Elijah has personal problems when his ex-wife decides to move to San Diego with their daughter. Patricia Arquette stars.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah and George begin a secret affair and meet at the cottage. However, Anna hopes that if she can push her father towards Sarah that he will be more open to learning about her relationship with Gino, but she is frustrated over the arrival of her aunt Regina. Meanwhile, James struggles with his emotions. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: George is asked to host a Japanese trade delegation on the eve of Remembrance Day. Knowing how the community will react, he asks Jack to come to the reception in the hope that the approval of a former Japanese prisoner of war might help deal with any ill feeling. At the event Regina is openly hostile toward Sarah, which she attributes to anti-Semitism. Anna's prolonged absence from Ash Park has Gino fearing that their relationship is over, but he is unaware that she is with Caroline awaiting the results of a pregnancy test. Meanwhile, James and Olivia continue to grow closer. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: A Small Price to Pay - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of three. Gina returns to her job immediately after receiving treatment for her cancer. Clearly unfit for work but too stubborn to admit it, she relies on June to oversee the relief. Gary is shot during a raid on a gang suspected of handling stolen goods, while Phil bristles with righteous indignation at the discovery that Kate has been mugged at knifepoint and vows to catch the thug responsible. As for Adam, he hits another snag as he tries to get his road safety initiative up and running, and again asks Isaac Collins to help - unaware his old friend's shady past is about to catch up with him.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The tension between Ric and Nick reaches crisis point during a bowel transplant operation, and Diane is forced to pick sides. Joseph treats a Bosnian woman who does not speak English, and is unaware of the full extent of her condition - but his decision to tell her the truth has disastrous consequences. After dealing with a medical emergency in the lab, Matt realises he misses treating patients.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: The arrival of arrogant new registrar Patrick Spiller makes a bad impression among staff, particularly Holly, who has crossed his path before. Adam receives a nasty reminder of Sam's accident, and the parachute jump proves eventful for a nervous Max. Claire Goose and Robert Gwilym star.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: A Small Price to Pay - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of three. Jack embarks on a relentless search for the gunman who shot Gary - and soon realises the finger of suspicion is pointing firmly at Adam's friend Isaac Collins. Smithy and Jonathan call at Gina's house to see why she hasn't turned up for work, and discover her unconscious on the bathroom floor. Away from the line of duty, Tony feels dejected at not having a date for Honey's birthday party and decides to hire an escort for the evening - little anticipating the surprise awaiting him.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Richard gets an unwelcome surprise from Cindy and Ian. Dot is in a happy mood as she looks forward to a special visitor. Nigel receives some bad news from Debbie via Clare.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Beth breaks Nigels weak and feeble heart and Michelle entrusts Shelly with Vicky, only to find out that shes exposed her daughter to a drug fuelled pot session.(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Bluewatch are called to extinguish a fire at a school and find two missing boys. Meanwhile, Adriane falls down a flight of stairs during an argument with Nick.(n)

programme.title: Monarch of the Glen
programme.desc: Archie has to choose between giving his business partnership with Paul one last chance or going mountaineering in Nepal with his sister Lizzie to honour their father's memory. Meanwhile, Molly is angry that Andrew has ignored her since returning from their romantic weekend, but Golly gains the courage to admit his hidden feelings to her. Alastair Mackenzie and Susan Hampshire star.(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Tom Beresford returns to Skelthwaite 20 years after a bitter family row, lured by the offer of a managerial position at Goddard's, and it's not long before he and his good-looking son are setting tongues wagging. Karen breaks all the rules to help two families come to terms with the consequences of a transplant operation, but her ill-advised actions backfire. Vic decides the time has come for a younger man to captain the Scorpions and an emotional Simon proposes to Anna. Drama, starring Tony Haygarth, Lesley Dunlop and Leslie Ash.(n)

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: Connie and Oswald join the holiday to see Billy and Aveline, and Joey's protest about the game shooting on the estate wins him an admirer.(n)

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Peggy becomes a Yellowcoat, but her happiness is spoilt when Alec Foster arrives with bad news. Last-ever episode of the hit comedy, starring Su Pollard and Ruth Madoc.(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean calls on Alistair's help to do a spot of match-making and Lionel finds himself in the middle of a muddle. Comedy, with Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The mischievous trio come to the aid of an old friend with an unusual medical problem, while Ivy becomes increasingly irate that no one will fix the cafe roof.(n)

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: Bacchus and Gently investigate a consignment of stolen passports, but the case takes an unexpected turn when a woman linked to the crime is found dead on the seashore with her mixed-race baby lying next to her. As the duo follow a lead on the murder victim's ex-boyfriend, they encounter racial prejudice and are drawn into an underground world of gang wars within the Arab community. Primeval's Andrew-Lee Potts guest stars.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The ex-coppers find themselves under the microscope when a psychologist arrives to conduct a study into older men in the workplace - but she soon reveals a more personal reason for her visit. It turns out that five years ago her brother, a cycle courier, was the victim of a hit-and-run that left him unable to recall anything prior to the incident. Now he says he was targeted for the parcel he was carrying, which was stolen at the scene - or so he believes. Sally Phillips guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The attempted suicide of a rape victim 12 years after her assault prompts Boyd to reopen the case, despite there being no new evidence. Their boss's illegal methods provide Grace, Spencer and the team with a suspect - but he quickly becomes aware the detectives are on his trail. Drama, starring Trevor Eve, with Ian Mercer, Michelle Dockery and Rory Kinnear.(n)

programme.desc: Elizabeth returns from hospital and is unimpressed with the way Regina is behaving towards George and asks her to leave, and Olivia and James decide that the best chance for their family lies in the city. Romantic drama, starring Noni Hazlehurst.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: George is delighted when Sarah agrees to marry him. When Elizabeth finds out about the proposal, she realises her threat to disinherit him has backfired - and decides to take a different approach. Elsewhere, Anna encounters complications with her pregnancy, and James and Olivia say goodbye to Harry before they leave Ash Park.(n)

A film crew turns up to shoot a promotional video, Connie makes her choice over the supervisor's position, and Kyla is forced to bring her son into work, while Justin faces up to what he really wants.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A gas explosion disrupts the Christmas cheer in Accident and Emergency, while Max and Tina don't need any mistletoe to make the most of being stuck in a lift. Barney tries his best to arrive at a diagnosis of seven dwarves who appear to have been poisoned, and a visiting choir lifts the spirits of the staff and patients. Claire Goose and Robert Gwilym star.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: A Small Price to Pay - Part Three
programme.desc: Part three of three. Adam learns the Borough Commander has been sent photos of him smoking marijuana, and decides to resign before he's unceremoniously thrown off the force. Rochelle continues her steamy illicit affair with Dan, while Jack questions Neil about his relationship with Suzie Sim and receives a bland assurance that it's all professional and above board - but later sees the pair in a compromising position.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Michelle and Sharon's past comes back to haunt them, Nigel wallows in self-pity, Dot's grandson begins to fall from grace, and Gita battles with Richard.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Shelly tries to arrange a boozy drug fuelled party filled with Walfords finest scallywags at Michelles house and Pauline ruins Arthurs chances to get his leg over with Mrs Hewett.(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Nick mounts a vigil at the unconscious Ariadne's bedside, while Hallam and George risk their necks to rescue a youngster trapped in the bedroom of a blazing house.(n)

programme.title: Monarch of the Glen
programme.desc: Lexie tries to run the estate in Archie's absence, but alienates Paul when she takes on more responsibility and makes matters worse when she agrees to sit on the board of a local distillery. Meanwhile, Duncan confronts Jess's ex-boyfriend and romance blossoms between Molly and Andrew, who plan to partition off a section of Glenbogle House so they can live together - breaking Golly's heart in the process. Susan Hampshire and Dawn Steele star.(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Vic makes a shock announcement about his future, Tom proves his worth in a crunch match for the Scorpions and Karen and Beth help reunite an estranged family after a baby girl goes missing. Drama, starring Leslie Ash and Kerrie Taylor.(n)

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: Adrian, who has been writing his memoirs, makes a decision that will change his life. Joey has reached the end of his tether.(n)

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: First episode of John Sullivan's comedy series, following the lives of Only Fools and Horses characters Boycie and Marlene. Denzil tells Boycie the infamous Driscoll Brothers are after the supergrass who had them jailed - prompting the car salesman to sell his business and move to rural Shropshire. John Challis and Sue Holderness star, with a guest appearance by Paul Barber.(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Judith's former boyfriend Alistair rocks the boat by asking Sandy for a date, and Jean is given cause to feel jealous of Lionel. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy organises a weekend away near Scarborough - but Clegg is convinced it will end in disaster as Sid, Ivy and Compo's nephew Gordon are accompanying them. Comedy, starring Brian Wilde and Peter Sallis.(n)

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detectives' case against a violent criminal is thrown into jeopardy by the murder of a key witness. Determined to secure a conviction, they immerse themselves in the seedy underworld of a seaside town, and as they press for answers, Havers is brought face to face with her childhood memories. Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small star.(n)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. When Eve finds evidence implicating her boyfriend, aid-worker Stefan, in murder, Boyd promises to support her and help get to the bottom of the mystery. But when it turns out her lover may also be involved in people smuggling, the pathologist becomes determined to prove his innocence, bringing her into conflict with her boss. Joseph Mawle guest stars, with Tara FitzGerald and Trevor Eve.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra is reunited with an old flame when DCI Larson of the Met's arts and antiques squad asks her to look into the murder of an antiques dealer who was not quite as legitimate as everyone thought. Brian is puzzled by Esther's odd behaviour and her new-found interest in the internet, leading him to think the unthinkable - she must be having an affair. Paul McGann and Jesse Birdsall guest star, with Amanda Redman and Alun Armstrong.(n)

However, a bigger shock is in store for those at Ash Park when a body is found in the river. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: An elderly Sarah reveals the truth about Bert Ford's death. Meanwhile in 1953, Sarah and Anna start their trial periods in their new homes, and Jack is furious at Carolyn for having kept their daughter a secret. Olivia becomes concerned over James's brutal hospital treatment and Elizabeth makes a shock announcement. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: A Small Price to Pay - Part Three
programme.desc: Part three of three. Adam learns the Borough Commander has been sent photos of him smoking marijuana, and decides to resign before he's unceremoniously thrown off the force. programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Nick rushes an operation so he can deal with a more glamorous case - a pole dancer with a rare condition - and impress Connie. However, his plan backfires and leaves him facing accusations of negligence. Donna finds out Justin went to a strip club when he said he was at his mother's, and Joseph treats a man who may have brain damage - an experience that brings back painful memories.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Max's day gets off to a celebratory start as he and Amanda announce their engagement, but he soon gets into trouble with his blushing bride-to-be when she discovers he's been secretly giving methadone to a heroin-overdose victim. Charlie and Jan fail to keep their personal and professional lives separate when they fall out over security arrangements, and a circulating e-mail soon outs their closet affair to the rest of Holby A&E. Robert Gwilym and Derek Thompson star.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Jack takes over as superintendent in Adam's absence, and promotes Neil Manson to acting DCI - despite grave misgivings about his recent conduct. Gabriel publicly asks Sheelagh out for dinner, allaying her fears that their relationship is over, and Ramani is devastated to learn Terry has been having an affair with his sister-in-law. Affairs of the heart also preoccupy Dan Casper, who receives another poison pen letter - but decides to ignore it and continue his affair with Rochelle.(n)

programme.desc: Jess fears she may be pregnant and Karen nervously prepares for a visit by one of David's old flames, the glamorous Evelyn Parrish. programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team investigates the mystery of a missing scientist, after the man's wife receives a series of emails from someone claiming to know what happened to him. The physics professor was last seen in 2004, on a train to Paris for a conference, and when he disappeared he was working on a potentially lucrative cold fusion project that had attracted attention from many large organisations. However, as the detectives dig deeper, a shadowy Whitehall figure warns Sandra's boss Strickland they should drop the case.(n)

programme.desc: As Knockout Week continues, the second group of eight amateurs compete for the chance to cook in a professional kitchen for the first time. First, the contestants are challenged to create a dish for the judges that they would have on the menu of their own restaurant, pop-up or feature in their cookbook. After this challenge, three of the group are sent home, and the remaining five head to Manchester to work in the flagship restaurant of 2015 MasterChef Champion, Simon Wood.(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Monsoon
programme.desc: The other side of the monsoon, as the winds bring dry air and drought. Budgerigars swarm in the Australian desert, gathering at a precious waterhole, and although birds of prey try to ambush them, they are mesmerised by the array of bright colours on show. On the island of Borneo, a bearded pig hunts for seafood on the beach, while in the Rajasthan desert, nomads haggle over the price of camels, which can provide vital transport during this lean season. The mugger crocodiles of Sri Lanka stalk the animals taking a much-needed drink and a young leopard learning to hunt discovers there is no such thing as an easy meal. Colin Salmon narrates.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the culinary delights of southern Spain, travelling from Valencia to Torremolinos and finding out about the influence of the Moors, who invaded the country in the early eighth century. Along the way, they visit one of the most fertile areas in Spain, get their hands on a freshly caught octopus, go for a mud bath and learn about flamenco, giving Dave a chance to show off his dance moves. Finally, they cook tapas for the San Juan midsummer night festival, where they reflect upon their entire Mediterranean adventure.(n)

programme.desc: Katie is annoyed that Devs is being misused for the amusement of Lyndon and Stewart, despite Forest and herself being willing to break the rules. On a flying visit to the 'Amaya' campus, Senator Laine warns Forest that he will face questions before an oversight committee. Meanwhile, concerns about Lily's state of mind are escalated, and Kenton tries to allay her fears, while being reassured by what he learns. Elsewhere, Jamie aims to make himself useful. Mystery thriller, starring Sonoya Mizuno and Jin Ha.(n)

programme.desc: Leslie, his best friend Cooper, father-in-law Chris and his mum's boyfriend Steve all go kayaking for his stag do. programme.desc: The presenter visits a women's prison in America that has adopted a radical new approach to incarceration based on giving the inmates a sense of purpose and self-esteem. She meets eight prisoners who are serving life sentences for murder, and discovers the complex sexual relationships that have developed among many of the inmates.(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams have reached the halfway point of their journey. For the next leg, must choose whether to travel through Bolivia or Chile - both countries with unstable political climates. Sam and Jo decide to break up their travel with regular breaks, while Jen and Rob have an explosive argument when they get caught up in Bolivia's general election. Emon and Jamiul find work in an Argentinian salt mine, and Dom has a health scare that may require him to pull out of the race.(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The final episode looks at radical new treatments and surgeries offering cancer patients hopes of survival, including two-year-old Yeshua, who has a tumour in his abdomen. In Iraq, where he was born, Yeshua was given a five per cent chance of survival. Though high doses of chemotherapy have shrunk the tumour over the past six months, in order for Yeshua to survive he needs an extremely complicated 12-hour operation at Alder Hey Children's Hospital to remove as much of the growth as possible.(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera

programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple hoping to relocate to rural Somerset on a budget of Â£700,000, looking for a home with enough land for animals and a glamping site. Away from the hunt, Alistair gets stuck in with some Cheddar making in the county where it originated.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return for a second visit to Lytham Hall in Lancashire, where items assessed include a chair believed to be from Nelson's flagship and a series of watercolours rescued from a skip and worth thousands. Geoffrey Munn finds a diamond pendant presented by the city of Liverpool, and there is some 1960s rock 'n roll memorabilia of the highest pedigree.(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Monsoon
programme.desc: Documentary examining the plants and animals that thrive in monsoon areas, narrated by Colin Salmon. Crested black macaques take advantage of an abundance of fruit, and maleo birds use the sun to incubate their eggs on the Indonesian island of Sulawesi. On Java, locals climb volcanoes to offer live sacrifices in thanks for the peaks' help in precipitating rain and fertilising plants with ash. The frequent and heavy rains in Borneo have destroyed the soils, created fierce competition for scarce nutrients, and driven evolution to produce some curious creatures. The Rajah Brooke's pitcher encourages tree shrews to relieve themselves to obtain the substances it needs, while the Bornean red leech is capable of swallowing the giant blue earthworm in one gulp.(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary Beard presents discussions and performances from people's homes, including an interview with Angel of the North sculptor Antony Gormley from his studio in Norfolk.(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Ten talented amateur cooks keen to reach the semi-final stage are challenged to make canapes or party food, with nine identical portions of a dish in miniature that can be either sweet or savoury. The stakes are high because at the end one contestants will be sent home and their dream of raising the 2020 trophy will be over. The remaining nine will embark on the next step of the competition. Presented by John Torode and Gregg Wallace.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers get back on their bikes to explore the culinary delights of several Mediterranean countries, discovering hearty and healthy food from France, Spain and Italy. They begin by searching for the authentic flavours of Italy, from simple local ingredients to spicy nduja pate and some of the best gelato around.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A truck driver is diverted along a narrow road that cannot cope with the weight of his vehicle, with disastrous consequences. Paramedics treat an unresponsive baby.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple from Surrey town to find a new home in the Welsh Borders, and also goes behind the scenes with conservation work at the Slimbridge Wetland Centre.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a bakery that produces 180,000 Cornish pasties a day, and discovers the very specific rule one of the pastries must comply with to genuinely be considered Cornish. Ruth Goodman debunks some myths about pasties and investigates the history of the pepper trade, while Cherry Healey learns about the science of onions - including why cutting them can bring tears to the eyes.(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Monsoon
programme.desc: Exploring the relationship that has developed over thousands of years between the people who live in monsoon regions and the wildlife they share them with. In the rice paddies of Assam, India, farmers battle a herd of hungry elephants over their crops, while on the Philippine island of Palawan, near Borneo, people still hunt in the forest and live in caves for part of the year. The programme also looks at the impact of organised religion, meeting members of the Bishnoi sect who believe that all life is sacred and have even been known to lay down their lives to protect trees. Colin Salmon narrates.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Curiosity
programme.desc: Martha and Lawrence from Sevenoaks take on Carol and Kath from the West Country in a challenge to curate the most valuable antiques collection. Presented by Paul.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Cookbook
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Simon King travel to the Germanic town of Swakopmund, on the Namibian coast, to experience a traditional barbecue - or braai. To find the right spot among the dunes, the motorcyclists take to the air in a microlight.(n)

programme.title: World Snooker: Crucible Classics
programme.desc: Jimmy White v Stephen Hendry. Hazel Irvine introduces action from the 1992 World Championship final, staged at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Caroline Hawley and Michael Baggott find objects at the University of Nottingham to take off to auction. Caroline comes across a jacket worn by Nottingham-born Olympic ice skater Jayne Torvill, and Michael gets excited by a 17th-century Bible that is hiding a secret. Host Paul Martin travels to north Nottinghamshire to visit the Portland Collection, which is one of the largest aristocratic art collections in Britain.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Comedian Shappi Khorsandi, former newsreader Michael Buerk, actress Amanda Abbington and comedian Elis James take part in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games. Their scores are tallied from across the week resulting in an overall champion being crowned.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Four groups of dedicated farmers who have shown resilience and ingenuity, go up against each other bidding to win this year's Against the Odds accolade at the Annual British Farming Awards. Narrated by Denis Lawson.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Road to Mexico
programme.desc: The chef retraces a road trip he took in 1968 down the Pacific Coast Highway to the Mexican border and beyond.He begins in San Francisco, the home of sourdough and where America's love affair with seasonal cooking took hold through restaurants like Chez Panisse, run by Alice Waters, who Rick is keen to meet. The chef also reflects on the time he had his first taste of Mexican food.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The latest two highest-scoring South West chefs cook their six-course menus again to impress judges Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The arbitrators are joined by comedian and children's author Nina Wadia, who has a special interest in cuisine for children with diabetes. Presented by Susan Calman.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: The green-fingered team guides team enthusiasts through seasonal highlights from across the country visiting stunning gardens, meeting the gardeners and finding out their secrets of success. Monty Don and faithful hounds Nigel and Nellie are at Longmeadow to bring inspiration as well as outline all the essential gardening jobs for this weekend. Co-presenter Rachel de Thame visits stunning gardens, meeting the gardeners and finding out their secrets of success.(n)

programme.title: Hubble: The Wonders of Space Revealed
programme.desc: Since its launch in April 1990, the Hubble space telescope has made more than 1.5 million observations of the universe. One of the most productive scientific instruments in history, over the past three decades it has transformed man's view of the cosmos. This documentary tells the craft's remarkable story, drawing upon contributions by the astronauts who spacewalked on Hubble to carry out critical repairs and upgrades and the astronomers who have used its unique powers to make new discoveries about the universe.(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: A self-isolated Nish Kumar hosts Britain's most uncompromising topical satire, tackling all the troubling news from a world that's put its faith in Boris Johnson and Donald Trump. His similarly quarantined correspondents, include Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen.(n)

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and comedy correspondents Rachel Parris, Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on everything that has - and hasn't - happened in the news.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Comprehensive coverage of the day's important national and international news stories.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Life of Crime (2013)
programme.desc: Two criminals kidnap the wife of a corrupt real-estate tycoon and demand a $1million ransom. However, the businessman is enjoying a holiday in the Bahamas with his mistress and does not want his wife back, setting in motion a complex series of double-crosses. Comedy based on Elmore Leonard's novel The Switch, starring Jennifer Aniston, Mos Def, Isla Fisher and Tim Robbins.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American painter Bob Ross takes on one of his greatest challenges as he paints majestic mountains looming over a colourful seascape in only half an hour.(n)

programme.title: The Beauty of Maps
programme.desc: Paul Barber of the British Library discusses the golden age of Dutch cartography and examines the importance of atlases to the commercial and creative endeavours of the 17th century. As maps became a means of expressing power, wealth and control, they grew increasingly elaborate and artistic - and the documentary visits the world's largest atlas, a 24-volume example covering just the Netherlands, and the Klencke Atlas, made for Charles II's restoration in 1660.(n)

programme.title: Michael Wood's Story of England
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the tale of the village of Kibworth in Leicestershire, detailing its status at various points in English history. He begins by examining the village's roots, using archaeology, landscape, language and DNA to create an impression of its first 1,000 years. He enlists the aid of local residents to dig 55 test pits, and excavates artefacts from the Roman era through to the Middle Ages.(n)

programme.title: King Arthur's Britain: The Truth Unearthed
programme.desc: With access to a major new excavation on the Tintagel peninsula in Cornwall, Professor Alice Roberts pulls together the latest evidence to reveal what Dark Age Britain was really like. Long known to have been a Dark Ages settlement, the new evidence reveals that Tintagel was also a seat of power, and Alice explores the link between the location and Arthurian legend, tracking down the early sources for the period and the first written reference to King Arthur.(n)

programme.title: Archaeology: A Secret History
programme.desc: Richard Miles charts the history of archaeological breakthroughs in a mission to understand the ancient past. In the first programme, he explores how the profession began by trying to prove a biblical truth.(n)

programme.title: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: Historian Janina Ramirez and art critic Alastair Sooke embark on cultural city breaks, offering insights into the most famous locations and uncovering hidden gems and untold stories to reveal how religion, revolution and trailblazing individuals can shape the art - and soul - of a metropolis. They begin by examining the art and design of the Dutch capital Amsterdam, along the way discovering how its culture reflects the liberal attitudes, appetite for global trade and love of home comforts that helped shape the character of this city.(n)

programme.title: Peggy Guggenheim: Art Addict (2015)
programme.desc: Profile of the socialite who became a central figure in the modern art movement, displaying the work of Jackson Pollock, Marcel Duchamp and her ex-husband Max Ernst, among others.(n)

programme.title: Dwarfs in Art: A New Perspective
programme.desc: Richard Butchins examines the representations of dwarfs in art and culture through the centuries, revealing society's shifting attitudes towards people with dwarfism.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American painter Bob Ross takes on one of his greatest challenges as he paints majestic mountains looming over a colourful seascape in only half an hour.(n)

programme.title: King Arthur's Britain: The Truth Unearthed
programme.desc: With access to a major new excavation on the Tintagel peninsula in Cornwall, Professor Alice Roberts pulls together the latest evidence to reveal what Dark Age Britain was really like. Long known to have been a Dark Ages settlement, the new evidence reveals that Tintagel was also a seat of power, and Alice explores the link between the location and Arthurian legend, tracking down the early sources for the period and the first written reference to King Arthur.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints a majestic snow-covered mountain, complete with rolling foothills, leafy trees, bushes and a serene lake.(n)

programme.title: The Beauty of Maps
programme.desc: An examination of how Fred Rose's 1877 Serio Comic Map of Europe at War allowed maps to take on a new direction and form, reflecting a changing world that meant they could be used as devices for humour, satire or story-telling. The graphic artist used a combination of creatures and human figures to represent each European nation, while in 1880 he was able to capture the mood of the British public with designs that commented on election issues featuring Gladstone and Disraeli.(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: How Britain Won the Space Race: The Story of Bernard Lovell and Jodrell Bank - Timeshift
programme.desc: The story of how Bernard Lovell turned a field in Cheshire into a key site in the space race. Lovell and his telescope at Jodrell Bank enabled the the Americans and the Russians to track their competing spacecraft during the height of the Cold War. This put Britain at the forefront of radio astronomy, a new science which transformed knowlegde of space and provided the key to understanding the beginnings of the universe - the big bang.(n)

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.sub-title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema: Coming of Age
programme.desc: The critic examines the coming-of-age film, and explains why it is such a powerful and popular medium for exploring the tumult and transformations of adolescence. Featured movies include The 400 Blows, The Breakfast Club, Kes and Lady Bird.(n)

programme.title: Lionel Richie at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive performances by the singer, including his first Top of the Pops appearance with the Commodores in 1979 and highlights from his 2009 concert at the BBC's Maida Vale studios.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jamie Bartlett investigates the impact of technology on democracy, revealing how aspects of social media designed to benefit the advertising industry by gathering personal data about users can be used to influence voters and spread misinformation. Featuring contributions from a key insider on Donald Trump's presidential campaign.(n)

programme.title: Novels That Shaped Our World
programme.desc: Documentary examining the impact of novels from three perspectives - the empire, class experience and, in this first film, women's voices. The programme aims to show the plight of women is a theme that reaches right back to the earliest novels, with extracts from Jane Austen, the Brontes, Mary Shelley and Virginia Woolf, through to the post-war publishing boom where a new generation of global writers such as Zadie Smith, Toni Morrison, Arundhati Roy and Margaret Atwood have continued to speak out for women to a new generation of readers.(n)

programme.title: Hollywood's Brightest Bombshell: The Hedy Lamarr Story (2017)
programme.desc: The life and career of the actress, who fled from her Nazi-sympathiser husband Friedrich Mandl in Austria and became a Hollywood star. Narrated by Diane Kruger.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints a majestic snow-covered mountain, complete with rolling foothills, leafy trees, bushes and a serene lake.(n)

programme.title: Lionel Richie at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive performances by the singer, including his first Top of the Pops appearance with the Commodores in 1979 and highlights from his 2009 concert at the BBC's Maida Vale studios.(n)

programme.title: Perfect Pianists at the BBC
programme.desc: David Owen Norris trawls through 60 years of BBC archive material to showcase some of the greatest names in the history of the piano. From the groundbreaking studio recitals of Benno Moiseiwitsch, Solomon and Myra Hess in the 1950s, through the legendary concerts of Vladimir Horowitz and Arthur Rubinstein to more recent performances including Alfred Brendel, Mitsuko Uchida and Stephen Hough, David celebrates some of the greatest players in a pianistic tradition that goes back to Franz Liszt in the 19th century.(n)

programme.title: Tunes for Tyrants: Music & Power with Suzy Klein
programme.desc: A exploration of music's crucial political role in the most turbulent years of the 20th century, beginning by looking at the years after the Russian Revolution and the First World War when music was seen as a tool to change society.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mark Goodier and Simon Parkin host the edition first shown on July 27, featuring performances by Bros, Simple Minds, Kirsty MacColl, the Primitives, Gloria Estefan, Inner City, Simply Red, Sonia and Alice Cooper.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the 70s 2
programme.desc: Performances by new wave bands from the BBC archives, including Elvis Costello, the Police, Ian Dury and the Blockheads, Squeeze, Blondie, Tom Petty and the Heartbreakers, the Cars, Patti Smith and Iggy Pop.(n)

programme.title: Queen: Rock the World
programme.desc: Documentary following the band as they recorded their sixth album, News of the World, and made plans for a tour of the US. Back in 1977, broadcaster Bob Harris was granted exclusive access, conducting interviews with all four members, as well as filming them at work in the studio, rehearsing their North American Tour. Until recently, the documentary was never completed and the footage lay unused in the archive. The film is supplemented by footage shot in the summer of 2017 as Brian May and Roger Taylor took Queen back to the US with Adam Lambert as lead singer.(n)

programme.title: Queen: The Legendary 1975 Concert
programme.desc: The rock band in performance at London's Hammersmith Odeon on Christmas Eve in 1975, featuring early singles including Liar, Keep Yourself Alive, Now I'm Here and Bohemian Rhapsody, as well as Brian May showcasing his guitar skills in Brighton Rock.(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: When Binky's grandparents miss his big soccer game, he decides to film a video reenactment with his friends, only things didn't quite happen the way Binky portrays them.(n)

programme.title: Elis James: Funny Nation
programme.desc: The stand-up explores the influence of Wales' different performance traditions on the nation's comedy and on its biggest stars. He discovers the lasting legacy of the popular 70s double act Ryan and Ronnie on some of today's biggest performers, finds out how the folk club scene helped produce Max Boyce and sees how Rhod Gilbert has inspired a new generation of performers - including the latest winner of the Edinburgh comedy award Jordan Brookes.(n)

programme.title: The Big Night In
programme.desc: Comic Relief and Children in Need come together for the first time to present an evening of entertainment the like of which has rarely been seen before, while raising funds for those on the frontline fighting Covid-19, as well as unsung heroes going the extra mile to support their communities during these unprecedented times. Lenny Henry and Matt Baker come together (while staying safely apart) to host the event, with help from Zoe Ball and Paddy McGuinness. Stars entertaining from the comfort of their homes include Gary Barlow, Peter Kay and Catherine Tate, and the stars of Strictly try to get viewers dancing around their living room. There'll also be some of the greatest comedy sketches of all time, as voted for by viewers, some incredible money-can't-buy prizes throughout the course of the evening.(n)

programme.sub-title: Percy am Mathan Ban (Percy the Polar Bear)
programme.desc: Following the activities of a 10-week-old polar bear being raised by his mother in the Canadian Arctic. Percy was born during the winter in a den kept up to 40 degrees warmer than outside, where the temperature drops to minus 60C and it is dark for months. Polar bears have thick coats, a 10cm thick layer of blubber that acts like thermal underwear, and huge fur-covered paws that are like snowshoes.(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets a hyperactive staffie called Cookie, who training staff believe could make an excellent working dog, but this is not usual for staffies, so finding someone to take him on may be difficult. Paul also meets a two-year-old Dogue de Bordeaux in need of some love after being found abandoned in a park, and nine-month-old teacup poodle Charlie is having trouble running with her broken leg. Head vet Shaun explains that Charlie has three breaks on one leg, and the best thing for her is going to be to remove it.(n)

programme.title: Our Queen at War
programme.desc: How the life of the young Princess Elizabeth was shaped by the events of the Second World War, and the extraordinary experiences she went through during her teenage years. The film draws on archive material and interviews with people who shared her experiences, and royal experts explore how she was transformed from a shy princess into Queen Elizabeth II and Britain's longest reigning monarch.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: As Britain and Europe remain locked down in the fight against Covid-19, Julie Etchingham asks how seriously the food supply chain is being affected. Crops will be ready to be harvested in the coming weeks, but there may be no labour available. She looks at how shops, supermarkets and delivery services are coping now the early panic buying is over. And she asks, if vulnerable families cannot get access to food at prices they can afford, will people ultimately be forced to change the way they eat?(n)

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show
programme.sub-title: The Martin Lewis Money Show - A Coronavirus Special
programme.desc: The financial concerns resulting from the coronavirus.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Driving Test
programme.desc: Grandmother Wendy is confident of passing but she is barely in the driving seat before nerves take hold. Seventeen-year-old boy racer Callum reckons he is more than test-ready, although his mum is not so sure, and Hull woman Shaunna returns after her failure in episode one. This time though she has a secret weapon and is determined to make it 13th time lucky. Narrated by Alan Carr.(n)

programme.title: Gordon, Gino and Fred: American Road Trip
programme.desc: For the final leg of their US jaunt, the trio arrive in Texas, a meat-lover's paradise where Gordon Ramsay is keen to introduce his companions to some of the best barbecue food known to man. In Lexington, Gordon turns lumberjack in an attempt to fell a dead oak - which is used to infuse ovens with wood smoke. There's a break for a bit of skinny dipping, before the trio fish go fishing, and stay in a strange cabin deep in the woods, surviving on beans and bourbon. They then hit the state capital of Austin, dressing as cowboys in the local bar and at the rodeo, before one last barbecue supper of slow-smoked beef brisket, pork ribs and spicy sausage.(n)

programme.desc: In Northern Ireland, James and local boy Paul Rankin brave the Carrick-a-Rede rope bridge together, then James cooks a tasty fish dish with the dramatic coastline as his backdrop. programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Young physiotherapist Lizzie Faulkner is shot dead while enjoying a holiday at a luxury country retreat. The resulting murder investigation unearths betrayal, guilt and the desire for atonement - as Vera moves quickly to avoid another tragedy, and Joe tries to make amends with the demons of his past. Crime drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, David Leon, Paul Ritter and Eva Quinn.(n)

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Michael Parkinson finds himself on the opposite side of the talk show format, as he discusses his life and career with Piers Morgan. The veteran host looks back on his encounters with some of the world's biggest stars, including Fred Astaire and Madonna, as well as what is perhaps his most famous moment, a powerful series of interviews with Muhammad Ali. He also reflects on his roots in a Yorkshire mining village, the death of his father, and his struggle with alcohol and the impact it had on his otherwise successful 60-year marriage.(n)

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Michael Parkinson finds himself on the opposite side of the talk show format, as he discusses his life and career with Piers Morgan. The veteran host looks back on his encounters with some of the world's biggest stars, including Fred Astaire and Madonna, as well as what is perhaps his most famous moment, a powerful series of interviews with Muhammad Ali. He also reflects on his roots in a Yorkshire mining village, the death of his father, and his struggle with alcohol and the impact it had on his otherwise successful 60-year marriage.(n)

programme.desc: Alan tells Tina to collect her belongings from the Woolpack. The day of Joseph's christening arrives, and Dave pleads with Kathy not to make him resign from Home Farm.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank suspects the Dingles have sabotaged his golf course. Tina confides in Nellie that she's never been dumped before, and Eric swindles Zak out of an antique chair.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Reg is all for replacing Maud with Tricia at the shop. Tracy is hurt when Deirdre doesn't visit her, while Ken continues to avoid the issue of Samir.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre prepares to fly Samir's body back to Morocco. Vera is sacked from Bettabuy, and Betty is stunned when Billy asks her to marry him.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: An unusual message appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local newspaper, revealing details of a forthcoming murder and, while at first glance it looks like a practical joke, the information proves to be deadly accurate. Miss Marple helps Inspector Craddock with his investigation and the pair soon uncover several likely suspects in the village. Feature-length drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Zoe Wanamaker, Elaine Paige, Virginia McKenna, Cherie Lunghi and Frances Barber.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Carol tries to help struggling single mum Barbara while dealing with the news that a woman claiming to be her own mother has arrived in Aidensfield. The nurse is then taken aback when Janet has the idea of reuniting them. Meanwhile, Mason's fears for Barbara are proved right after a shocking turn of events, and Wetherby and Younger enlist Peggy to search for the chief constable's missing dog.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A college dean is found bludgeoned to death in local woodland, following a heated pub debate. When an alchemic image is discovered by the body, denoting the first of four stages of a process called 'The Magnum Opus', the detectives realise that there are three more murders to follow. Before any more crimes are committed, the team must uncover the guilty secret that the victims share in order to learn the identity of the killer and the potential victims.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.sub-title: Cold Is the Grave - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective is drawn into the criminal underworld of Soho when he reluctantly accepts an unofficial request from Chief Superintendent Rydell to find his missing daughter. Meanwhile, an armed robbery in Eastvale turns into a murder investigation after the perpetrator is found dead in his home and when a battered body is found in a local nightclub, the identity of the victim leads Banks and Cabbot to a devastating realisation about both cases.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.sub-title: Cold Is the Grave - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. A surveillance operation establishes a clear link between the murders of Charles McKay and Barry Clough and the subsequent investigation takes a number of alarming twists before uncovering a conspiracy pointing to one of the team being an informer. As Banks pushes all the suspects hard, the case threatens to undermine his integrity as an officer and brings his relationship with Cabbot to breaking point.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: The Darling Buds of May - Part One
programme.desc: The first episode of the whimsical rural drama based on HE Bates' novels. Tax collector Cedric Charlton visits the Larkin household intent on getting the head of the family - Pop - to correctly fill out his tax forms. In so doing he falls for attractive daughter Mariette. Starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Gloomy new tenant Mr Gray moves into Rigsby's boarding house, but proves so depressed that he barely seems to notice the dingy surroundings, and keeps talking about 'going to a better place' - raising fears he is contemplating suicide. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter and David Swift.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: An unusual message appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local newspaper, revealing details of a forthcoming murder and, while at first glance it looks like a practical joke, the information proves to be deadly accurate. Miss Marple helps Inspector Craddock with his investigation and the pair soon uncover several likely suspects in the village. Feature-length drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Zoe Wanamaker, Elaine Paige, Virginia McKenna, Cherie Lunghi and Frances Barber.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason visits a travellers' camp to investigate attacks on two hippies, and learns one of them had recently clashed with a local gamekeeper - who is later found murdered. Meanwhile, David suspects his taxi has been sabotaged, and Wetherby begins following his wife - only to discover she is being unfaithful. Joe McFadden and David Lonsdale star.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The bailiffs arrive at the Dingles'. Linda tells Biff she doesn't deserve him, and Frank tries to stop Chris being arrested.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank is upset that everyone seems to be on the Dingles' side. The villagers begin donating food to the Dingles, while Frank and Kim are made to feel unwelcome at the Woolpack.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera decides she doesn't want to work anymore. Tracy blames Ken for the mess they're all in, and Steve is attacked by an assailant with a baseball bat.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim doesn't tell Liz the truth about what happened to Steve. Denise advises Fiona to leave Steve before he drags her down with him, and he is forced to tell Vicky about his debts.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan as the genteel sleuth. A woman experiences an unsettling vision after moving into the home of her dreams - and it transpires she lived in the house as a child. Miss Marple investigates the history of the property and discovers it was connected with a baffling disappearance years before - which has never been solved. With Dawn French, Russ Abbot, Geraldine Chaplin, Phil Davis and Sarah Parish.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason visits a travellers' camp to investigate attacks on two hippies, and learns one of them had recently clashed with a local gamekeeper - who is later found murdered. Meanwhile, David suspects his taxi has been sabotaged, and Wetherby begins following his wife - only to discover she is being unfaithful. Joe McFadden and David Lonsdale star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Dr Ellingham visits an elderly patient - but ends up having to contend with her irate son. To make matters worse, the man turns out to be one of Louisa's ex-boyfriends. However, trouble is not just confined to the doctor's daily routine as the villagers find themselves forced to deal with the threat of a mystery mugger. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes as the grumpy GP.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa is eager to be promoted to head teacher, but her chances are threatened by a spate of sickly children and a frosty relationship with the board of governors, including a surly Martin. Mrs Cronk scalds herself at the fish and chip shop and has to put Bert in charge. To everyone's amazement, the business does a roaring trade - but Al suspects his father is cutting corners.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective receives a disturbing phone message from estranged brother Roy and heads to Harrogate to find him. Meanwhile at a murder scene, the team is introduced to new recruit Helen Morton, who quickly alienates her colleagues with her tactless style. The officer then makes matters worse when she finds evidence linking Banks to the victim's body and speculates he could be a suspect. Unaware everyone is searching for him, the DCI begins to delve into Roy's life and soon uncovers questionable business transactions and associates. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Caroline Catz (Doc Martin) and Andrea Lowe.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The detective visits his childhood home to break the news of Roy's death, and his parents plead with him to find out what happened. Defying Morton, Banks returns to the station in an attempt to lead the investigation into the murders of his brother and Jennifer Lewis, and both officers are convinced the key to cracking the cases lies with a woman being treated at a clinic. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson and Caroline Catz.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim feels unable to relax with the Rachids sharing his home. Betty gives Raquel dancing lessons, and Tracy suggests to Samir that they cancel the operation and wait for a donor.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alf is stunned when he's awarded an OBE. Tracy meets Daniel for the first time, Maud warms to Tricia when she stands up to her, and Samir is attacked by a gang of youths.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe is worried when Linda is late for work. Kathy confronts Kim with what Dave has told her about their affair, and Linda takes an overdose.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jan and Ned wait for news on Linda, who has been admitted to intensive care. Tina flirts with Vic in front of Viv, and Ned forces his way into the Weirs' house to confront Danny.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An encounter with a gang of armed men presents Miller with a difficult dilemma that puts his professional and personal future in jeopardy. Mason and Wetherby struggle to deal with student protesters who are furious at the presence of a suspected Nazi collaborator in the town, and the situation escalates further when an attempt is made on the man's life. John Duttine and Joe McFadden star.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger insists on leading the team convoy of cars to a fixture in the country but his map-reading skills let him down en route, and a run-in with a troublesome tractor driver ensures the day ends dismally. Richard Harris's comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Josie Lawrence and Timothy Spall.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Mim tries to persuade Roger to cancel a match with Piggy Pearson's team because of an incident with the latter's divorced sister. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby returns from a dismal holiday in Spain to find a surprise awaiting him - the love of his life, Miss Jones, is back - with a baby in tow. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A notorious burglar escapes from prison and returns to Aidensfield to pursue a vendetta against Ashfordly police. Roping in two local boys, the villain dupes them into helping him carry out his plan while PC Mason desperately tries to find him before he strikes again. Dawn sets Joyce up on a date with PC Younger - but is oblivious to the fact that the young copper actually has feelings for her. George Cole guest stars, with Joe McFadden and John Duttine.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Linda is told by the doctors she is lucky to be alive. Kim is furious about Kathy being Joseph's godmother, and Tina announces a Save The Dingles fundraiser at the Woolpack.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Terry is annoyed with Tina for not telling him about the fundraiser. Sarah wants Jack to apologise to Ned, and Nellie writes to John Major, the Queen and Terry Wogan for help.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The police call on Deirdre and take her to the hospital. She is stunned when the doctors tell her Samir isn't going to recover, and she must give consent for the kidney to be taken.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tracy comes round from her operation. Deirdre doesn't know how to tell Samir's mother that he's dead, and Ken doesn't let Tracy know what's happened to Samir.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, Pam Ferris, Niamh Cusack, John Hannah, Amanda Holden, Griff Rhys Jones, David Warner, Jenny Agutter and Rob Brydon.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A notorious burglar escapes from prison and returns to Aidensfield to pursue a vendetta against Ashfordly police. Roping in two local boys, the villain dupes them into helping him carry out his plan while PC Mason desperately tries to find him before he strikes again. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Millionaire Gordon Cloade's wife is widowed for the second time at the tender age of 23, arousing the suspicions of his impoverished relatives. Fanning the flames of envy and hatred, psychic aunt Kathy Cloade reveals the grieving widow's first husband is in fact still alive, and a second death prompts Poirot to delve deeper into the family's dark secrets. David Suchet stars, with Jenny Agutter, Tim Piggot-Smith and Celia Imrie.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.sub-title: Friend of the Devil - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Cabbot investigates when a woman is found murdered on the moors. As the identity of the victim is established, it becomes apparent any number of suspects may have had a reason to want her dead. Meanwhile, Banks is called in when the body of a teenage girl is discovered in a storage room down an alleyway. Stephen Tompkinson and Andrea Lowe star.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.sub-title: Friend of the Devil - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. The detective and his team find themselves investigating the murder of one of their own. Cabbot is convinced there is a link between the deaths of Lucy Payne and her colleague, but is dismissed as trying to push her career ambitions. However, after receiving new information from another force, she begins to uncover the real connection between the killings. Andrea Lowe stars.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, Pam Ferris, Niamh Cusack, John Hannah, Amanda Holden, Griff Rhys Jones, David Warner, Jenny Agutter and Rob Brydon.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby discovers there is a new man in Miss Jones's life and decides to declare his feelings for her - but his romantic gesture comes too late. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington.(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: It's the day of Kevin and Maggie's wedding - but they're nowhere to be seen. As the members of the congregation fidget, Clive makes an accusation against Roger. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Miss Jones falls for new guest Hilary, a budding playwright who sends Rigsby into fits of jealousy by offering her the starring role in his latest masterpiece. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter and guest Peter Bowles.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby splashes out on a new sports car in the hope of impressing Miss Jones, but a cruel practical joke by Philip convinces him he has run over his feline friend Vienna. Leonard Rossiter and Frances de la Tour star.(n)
