programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera decides she doesn't want to work anymore. Tracy blames Ken for the mess they're all in, and Steve is attacked by an assailant with a baseball bat.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim doesn't tell Liz the truth about what happened to Steve. Denise advises Fiona to leave Steve before he drags her down with him, and he is forced to tell Vicky about his debts.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan as the genteel sleuth. A woman experiences an unsettling vision after moving into the home of her dreams - and it transpires she lived in the house as a child. Miss Marple investigates the history of the property and discovers it was connected with a baffling disappearance years before - which has never been solved. With Dawn French, Russ Abbot, Geraldine Chaplin, Phil Davis and Sarah Parish.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason visits a travellers' camp to investigate attacks on two hippies, and learns one of them had recently clashed with a local gamekeeper - who is later found murdered. Meanwhile, David suspects his taxi has been sabotaged, and Wetherby begins following his wife - only to discover she is being unfaithful. Joe McFadden and David Lonsdale star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Dr Ellingham visits an elderly patient - but ends up having to contend with her irate son. To make matters worse, the man turns out to be one of Louisa's ex-boyfriends. However, trouble is not just confined to the doctor's daily routine as the villagers find themselves forced to deal with the threat of a mystery mugger. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes as the grumpy GP.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa is eager to be promoted to head teacher, but her chances are threatened by a spate of sickly children and a frosty relationship with the board of governors, including a surly Martin. Mrs Cronk scalds herself at the fish and chip shop and has to put Bert in charge. To everyone's amazement, the business does a roaring trade - but Al suspects his father is cutting corners.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective receives a disturbing phone message from estranged brother Roy and heads to Harrogate to find him. Meanwhile at a murder scene, the team is introduced to new recruit Helen Morton, who quickly alienates her colleagues with her tactless style. The officer then makes matters worse when she finds evidence linking Banks to the victim's body and speculates he could be a suspect. Unaware everyone is searching for him, the DCI begins to delve into Roy's life and soon uncovers questionable business transactions and associates. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Caroline Catz (Doc Martin) and Andrea Lowe.(n)

programme.title: Tommy Cooper
programme.desc: Patrick Cargill and Trisha Noble join the celebrated comedian and entertainer as he performs a variety of sketches, and a few of magic tricks.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The coroner examines a suspicious plane crash at sea and through the course of his investigation, discovers the victims died needlessly.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo investigates a bombing on a restaurant, unaware that one of his fellow detectives lost his secret mistress in the blast - and whose unstable wife may be responsible for the attack.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar investigates a people smuggling ring, focusing his inquiries on the activities of a local yacht club. When it is revealed that one of the immigrants has gone on the run while suffering from smallpox, the Saint sets out to locate him before he can spread the disease.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur excludes Terry from his latest business venture, prompting him to embark on a search for alternative employment. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A captured British spy is released when his Russian counterpart falls into British hands. C15 is tasked with ensuring his safety as well as establishing whether his information is genuine. However, such intentions fail miserably when he is poisoned. Bodie and Doyle set out to determine what part his daughter, a concert pianist, played in his death - assuming she really is his daughter.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan and Carter discover a road accident victim was a gang member involved in a wages robbery, and Regan is determined to nail the modern-day Bonnie and Clyde who have already made fools of the police. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: Sam develops a new forensic technique to help police track down murderers, but everyone else in the department seems strangely uninterested.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A convicted murderer exploits a legal loophole to get a second trial - and may walk free when the key witness is murdered. Guest starring Sharon Gless (Cagney & Lacey, Burn Notice).(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar agrees to act as go-between, taking ransom money to the kidnappers of the wayward daughter of a rich film producer. However, he has a trick up his sleeve - quite literally - in the shape of a camera disguised as a wrist-watch, so even though he is blindfolded, he can later retrace his steps. Roger Moore directs and stars.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.sub-title: The Long Ride Back to Scratchwood
programme.desc: An ex-footballer persuades Arthur to buy 2,000 tickets for an England v Scotland football match. However, trouble ensues once it becomes apparent they are forgeries. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A ministerial secretary innocently leaks information regarding a secret peace talks meeting to her lover, leading to a shooting incident and placing the future of the summit in jeopardy. While her boyfriend's involvement remains in doubt, Cowley gets her to pass on false information in the hope it will prove his innocence or confirm his guilt. Drama, starring Gordon Jackson, Lewis Collins and Martin Shaw.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A business tycoon is blackmailed for his part in a massacre while serving in the Army 25 years earlier. However, his problems are compounded after he seeks help from his former commanding officer. Colin Jeavons guest stars, with John Thaw.(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: How traditional seaside rock is made, a process that has not changed since Victorian times. Plus, how a new fire extinguisher is saving lives around the world. Jimmy Nail narrates.(n)

programme.title: The Car Years
programme.desc: Vicki Butler-Henderson and Alex Riley choose their favourite car from 1964, then try to convince the expert panel of judges that their choice is best.(n)

programme.title: The Car Years
programme.desc: Alex Riley and Vicki Butler-Henderson choose their favourite car from 1984, then try to convince the panel of judges that their choice is best.(n)

programme.title: Rocky III (1982)
programme.desc: World heavyweight boxing champion Rocky Balboa settles down into a life of luxury - but a cloud appears on the horizon in the shape of ambitious contender Clubber Lang, whose brutal fighting style represents a serious threat to his title. Drama, starring Sylvester Stallone, Mr T, Burgess Meredith, Talia Shire, Burt Young, Carl Weathers and Hulk Hogan.(n)

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news.(n)

programme.title: Ali (2001)
programme.desc: Biopic of champion boxer Muhammad Ali, covering events from his 1964 fight with Sonny Liston to his Rumble in the Jungle with George Foreman 10 years later. Along the way, the fighter converts to Islam, maintains a conflicted friendship with Malcolm X and causes controversy with his refusal to fight in the Vietnam War. Directed by Michael Mann and starring Will Smith, Jamie Foxx, Jon Voight and Mario Van Peebles.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: Miraculous claims are made about an ancient skeleton in Mexico, so Quincy and Sam are sent to study it, but are disturbed by a tabloid journalist's take on the story.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A serial killer terrorises Manhattan, targeting those who have wronged him financially. A graffiti artist confesses to the crimes, but Theo has his doubts about the man's story.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: The thief encounters a deposed king-turned-crooked gambler.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A wealthy family insists Dr Sudbury, a hypnotist friend of Arthur's, has mesmerised its teenage daughter Rita into agreeing to marry him. Arthur finds himself immersed in the fracas, while simultaneously trying to take over the affairs of a steam-cleaning business. Light-hearted drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: An old adversary of Doyle's stirs up rivalry between factions of political terrorist group the Organization as a diversion in order to launch a revenge attack on the CI5 operative, who he blames for the death of his brother. Action drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The son of an ex-informant is kidnapped and the Flying Squad is brought in to assist in the hunt. Against Regan's advice his father, a wealthy businessman, pays the ransom and the boy is returned. However, the reunion quickly turns sour. Joss Ackland guest stars, with John Thaw.(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: Jimmy Nail narrates a look inside a firework factory, before a lesson in pork pies in Melton Mowbray. Plus, how respective experts create fishing rods and single malt whisky.(n)

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney are joined by England international angler Matt Godfrey in the Cotswolds, where they target 5lb tench at an idyllic lake, before heading to the Gloucester canal late at night in search of a 10lb zander, otherwise known as 'the vampire fish'.(n)

programme.title: Rocky IV (1985)
programme.desc: The Italian Stallion agrees to help former champion Apollo Creed train for a match against a seemingly invincible boxer from the Soviet Union, only to see his friend die as a result of injuries sustained in the fight. Rocky vows to avenge him, and prepares to take on the Russian heavyweight in his own country. Drama sequel, directed by and starring Sylvester Stallone. With Dolph Lundgren, Talia Shire, Carl Weathers and Brigitte Nielsen.(n)

programme.title: Bear's Mission
programme.desc: Adventurer Bear Grylls teaches the accomplished boxer some alternative survival skills. The duo start off on the notorious Commando Ridge in Cornwall, where the boxer has to climb granite rock, alpine traverses and sharp pinnacles. Next, Bear surprises AJ with a tricky descent - a major challenge for anyone nervous of heights - with a 'death drop' on all sides. When it comes to nutrition, Bear unveils a rotted sheep's leg, advising Anthony they must eat the maggots while hacking in to get the marrow.(n)

programme.title: Car Crash Global: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Documentary featuring dash-cam video footage of a host of astonishing accidents, spectacular smashes and enthralling escapes from Scandinavia and China.(n)

programme.title: The Americans
programme.desc: Philip and Elizabeth come together for a perilous operation, and Stan and Henry spend some quality time together.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The crime-busting coroner helps defend a college sports coach accused of indirectly causing the death of a potential Olympic champion.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo tries to stop a child molester with diplomatic immunity before he strikes again, but the FBI are determined to avoid an international incident. Guest starring Hector Elizondo.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Mysterious events unfold involving a beautiful blonde, and Templar gets behind the wheel of a rally car. Last-ever episode of the vintage adventure series, starring Roger Moore.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur's car lot is threatened with closure, spurring him to enter the cut-and-thrust world of local politics to have more of a say in local issues. Comedy drama, starring Dennis Waterman, George Cole and Lennard Pearce, best remembered as Grandad in Only Fools and Horses.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle spring into action when East German agents blackmail a government employee into revealing secret plans for a new type of jet-engine tank. Featuring an appearance by playwright Lynda La Plante, alongside stars Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The Flying Squad trails diamond thief Jimmy Fleet on the inside, with Carter acting undercover as a fellow prisoner aiming to gain his confidence. On his release for lack of evidence, Fleet seeks Carter's assistance after discovering his girlfriend has vanished with the jewels, suspecting she has been abducted. Michael Elphick guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look behind the scenes at the Bombay Sapphire distillery to discover how exotic botanicals from around the world are used to flavour this classic British gin.(n)

programme.title: The Car Years
programme.desc: Vicki Butler-Henderson and Alex Riley champion cars from 1955. Vicki chooses the Mercedes 190SL, while Alex plumps for the British MGA sports car.(n)

programme.title: The Car Years
programme.desc: Vicki Butler-Henderson and Alex Riley choose cars from 1980. Vicki likes the Audi Quattro, while Alex goes for a Renault 5 Turbo.(n)

programme.title: Rocky V (1990)
programme.desc: The heavyweight champion is forced into retirement due to brain damage inflicted in the ring, and finds his fortune has been lost on the stock market by his accountant. Returning to his working-class roots, he trains an up-and-coming fighter, only to see his protege turn against him. Drama sequel, starring Sylvester Stallone, Talia Shire and real-life boxer Tommy Morrison.(n)

As Britain increasingly looks beyond politics to science for reassurance about how and when the crisis will end, this programme brings together experts to take part in a straight-talking, fact-based discussion providing scientific answers to the most difficult questions. The discussion covers the psychology of isolation, the technical issues behind mass production of ventilators, and, crucially, gives viewers the chance to explore the fascinating science behind this extraordinary outbreak.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity 5 Go Barging
programme.desc: Penny Smith, Tony Christie, Tessa Sanderson, Diarmuid Gavin and Tom Conti tackle a new waterways adventure on the Canal du Midi, a 17th-century feat of engineering. They find their new boats at the marina in Sete on the coast of the Mediterranean. The banter begins with their first decision over who gets which boat, resulting in repelling of boarders, cauliflower cannonballs and the confiscation of keys. The famous five tangle with local fishermen and have confused communication with bridge controllers, but eventually leave port and set off across France's largest lagoon, the Etang du Thau.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity 5 Go Barging
programme.desc: Penny Smith, Tony Christie, Tessa Sanderson, Diarmuid Gavin and Tom Conti continue their journey along the Canal du Midi, though a strike by French lock keepers means that they find themselves in a race against time to get through the formidable Fonserannes staircase flight, or risk being stuck for days. Once at the flight, they discover there is a dangerous and extreme side to canal life as they dramatically crash the boats in the lock, resulting in a man-overboard fright. The quintet then head for their final mooring place - a picturesque village in a famed wine region.(n)

programme.title: Jo Brand's Cats & Kittens

programme.title: Secrets of Your Supermarket Food
programme.desc: Sian Williams and Stefan Gates get to the heart of supermarket food - the ingredients - finding out why what appears on the front of the packet may not always be the same as what's inside. Stefan highlights the use of cheap ingredients by comparing pizzas made with real cheese and processed cheese. Can a consummate pizza chef tell the difference? Sian looks at the amount of fat in ready meals and Stefan reveals how ice-cream companies use the cheapest secret ingredient of them all - air.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Royals on Tour
programme.desc: Second of two documentaries going behind the scenes with the royal family on their various travels. Through a combination of archive footage and interviews with insiders, the films reveal the significant historic occasions, most surprising secrets and incredible moments when the royals went globetrotting.(n)

programme.title: The QE2
programme.sub-title: The QE2: Queen of the Seas
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the history of the liner, which was one of the most influential maritime designs in British history. Featuring interviews with the QE2's crew and the workers who built her, cameras reveal the story behind the strikes and the looting epidemics that almost spelled the end of the ship before she was even finished.(n)

programme.title: The Missing Evidence
programme.sub-title: Jack the Ripper: The Missing Evidence
programme.desc: The identity of one of history's most notorious serial killers has generated many theories over the years since the three-month period in 1888 when a number of women were murdered on the streets of Whitechapel. Journalist Christer Holmgren believes that he has found a suspect who can not only be linked directly to one of the murders, but also whose daily routine could be consistent with all the other deaths. Having studied the case for more than three decades, his focus eventually fell on a witness called Charles Cross, whose real name turned out to be Charles Allen Lechmere, and he found a mass of incriminating evidence.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity 5 Go Barging
programme.desc: Journalist Michael Buerk, actors Shaun Williamson and Amanda Barrie, singer Anita Harris and politician John Prescott unite for the gentlest of jaunts along Britain's most beautiful waterways, heading for the heart of Shakespeare country. After meeting and moving into twin narrowboats, the quintet discovers that there is a lot to learn about life on Caldon Canal in Staffordshire. Not only must they adjust to life in cramped conditions, but they must also learn how to steer a 60-foot hunk of floating metal. Despite both having Navy experience, John and Shaun keep crashing the barges and getting stuck.(n)

programme.title: Digging Up Britain's Past
programme.desc: Archaeologists and presenters Raksha Dave and Alex Langlands explore more fascinating periods in British history through archaeological digs. In this instalment, the pair join a team from Reading University at a site in Silchester, near Reading, as they examine one of the best-preserved Roman sites in Britain.(n)

programme.title: Walking Britain's Lost Railways
programme.desc: Rob Bell explores the Waverley Route, which ran south from Edinburgh for 100 miles through the Scottish Borders to Carlisle, connecting with what is now known as the West Coast Main Line. After years of campaigning, the first stage of Rob's journey is aboard the new Borders Railway, the longest railway to open in over a century. It follows part of the old Waverley Route, making fine use of the 170-year-old, 23-arch Newbattle Viaduct. Rob also pays a visit to one of Hawick's surviving cashmere factories and discovers a First World War prisoner of war camp served by the railway.(n)

programme.title: Around the World by Train with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: The actor enjoys a 10,000km journey along the Trans-Siberian Railway, a network of railways connecting Moscow with the Russian Far East. In Khabarovsk, Tony is met by the extraordinary sight of the River Amur, frozen solid. He's invited to warm up over dinner with a group of locals who offer him a taste of their home-brewed vodka. Tony then re-joins the rails, learning about how Russia has changed since the days of communism. Further stops on his tour are Yekaterinburg, in the foothills of the Ural Mountains and Red Square in Moscow, before he heads west towards Finland's capital city, Helsinki.(n)

programme.title: Stanley Baxter's Best Bits...and More
programme.desc: Documentary profiling the multiple Bafta-winning entertainer, from his early days as a child actor in Glasgow, through his long and celebrated career in radio, theatre, television and films, to the present day. The programme focuses primarily on his golden years in the 1960s and 70s, when his many TV impressions - particularly the Queen (aka the Duchess of Brendagh) - were famous throughout Britain.(n)

programme.title: TV's Funniest Ever Sketches
programme.desc: A celebration of the very best of comedy sketches by some of the greatest performers ever to grace British television screens. Featuring The Breakfast Sketch from Morecambe & Wise, Slade on Holiday from The Smell of Reeves and Mortimer, Going for an English from Goodness Gracious Me, The Class Sketch from The Frost Report and many more. Presented by John Thomson.(n)

programme.title: Houseboat (1958)
programme.desc: An Italian socialite rebels against her domineering father and gets a job as a maid for a widowed father. While helping her employer learn to understand his three precocious children, she also falls in love with him and must decide whether or not to reveal her secret. Romantic comedy, starring Cary Grant and Sophia Loren.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Nurse (1959)
programme.desc: The patients on the men's ward of Haven Hospital cause more mayhem for the staff than the rest of the hospital combined, forcing the management to send in a formidable matron to control the situation. Feeling aggrieved by her no-nonsense methods, the chaps decide the time is right to get their own back. Comedy, with Kenneth Williams, Hattie Jacques, Joan Sims, Kenneth Connor, Charles Hawtrey, Shirley Eaton and Leslie Phillips.(n)

programme.title: All That Heaven Allows (1955)
programme.desc: A wealthy New England widow breaks all the taboos of her conservative social circle by falling madly in love with her younger gardener. Her friends and grown-up children are scandalised as she contemplates marrying him, leaving her with a dilemma - to either bow to convention or take a chance at being happy. Romantic drama, with Jane Wyman, Rock Hudson, Agnes Moorehead and Conrad Nagel.(n)

programme.title: Baggage Claim (2013)
programme.desc: An air hostess is horrified at the thought of her younger sister getting married while she is still single. She realises her job gives her access to eligible men from across America, and begins clocking up thousands of air miles as she scours the country for a potential fiance. Romantic comedy, with Paula Patton and Taye Diggs.(n)

programme.title: Wonder (2017)
programme.desc: A 10-year-old boy with facial disfigurements that have so far required almost 30 operations takes on a whole new challenge when, after being home educated his entire life, enters mainstream school for the first time. As he struggles to fit in, his fellow pupils and the community are forced to learn the power of compassion and acceptance. Julia Roberts, Owen Wilson and Jacob Tremblay star in Stephen Chbosky's drama, based on the best-selling novel by RJ Palacio.(n)

programme.title: Midnight Run (1988)
programme.desc: When accountant Jonathan Mardukas skips bail after having been arrested for embezzlement, bounty hunter Jack Walsh is hired to catch the fraudster and return him from New York to Los Angeles. Unfortunately, his task is hindered by the ingenuity of Mardukas and the attentions of pursuing FBI agents and Mafia hitmen. Comedy thriller, starring Robert De Niro, Charles Grodin, Yaphet Kotto, Dennis Farina, John Ashton and Joe Pantoliano.(n)

programme.title: Ashby (2015)
programme.desc: As part of a school assignment, student Ed Wallis gets to know his neighbour Ashby Holt, an ex-CIA assassin with a personal secret. Comedy drama, with Mickey Rourke, Nat Wolff, Emma Roberts and Sarah Silverman.(n)

programme.title: Kaleidoscope (2016)
programme.desc: A man just been released from prison attempts to get on with his life, but the reappearance of his domineering mother scuppers his plans and unearths deep-seated trauma. Psychological thriller, starring Toby Jones and Anne Reid.(n)

programme.title: Heaven Knows, Mr Allison (1957)
programme.desc: A US marine corporal stationed on a South Pacific island and a novice nun on a mission are forced into the hills when Japanese forces arrive and set up military bases during the Second World War. Before long the pair develop strong emotional ties, but in resisting their feelings for each other the relationship becomes one of deep mutual respect. Romantic drama, written and directed by John Huston, with Robert Mitchum and Deborah Kerr.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Regardless (1961)
programme.desc: A businessman starts up an odd-job agency, but employs seven hopeless handymen incapable of completing any task without causing utter chaos. Matters come to a head when their landlord tries to evict them - forcing the bungling workmen to finally prove their worth. Slapstick comedy, starring Sid James, Charles Hawtrey and Kenneth Williams.(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Show on Earth (1952)
programme.desc: A circus owner hires a celebrated trapeze artist to perform with his girlfriend as the star attraction - leading to bitter rivalry in the air and sexual tension on the ground. Meanwhile, a clown tries to keep his past a secret, and racketeers prepare to muscle in on the concessions. Cecil B DeMille's Oscar-winning drama, starring Charlton Heston, Cornel Wilde, Betty Hutton and James Stewart.(n)

programme.title: X-Men: Days of Future Past (2014)
programme.desc: In a future ravaged by war, the superhero team makes a desperate last stand against a force of deadly robots. Their only hope is to send Wolverine back in time to the 1970s, to join forces with an earlier version of the X-Men and change the events that started the conflict. Superhero adventure sequel uniting the casts of the original movie and prequel First Class, starring Hugh Jackman, Jennifer Lawrence, James McAvoy and Michael Fassbender.(n)

programme.title: Transformers: The Last Knight (2017)
programme.desc: The shape-changing robots and their human allies face a confrontation with their original creator, who has brainwashed their leader and intends to save her own home planet by destroying Earth. To save the human race they must recover an ancient artefact from Arthurian legend - but their mission is threatened by old enemies as they uncover a terrible secret about the history of the world. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Mark Wahlberg, Anthony Hopkins, Gemma Chan, Stanley Tucci and John Turturro.(n)

programme.title: Lowlife (2017)
programme.desc: A ruthless LA crime boss connects three down-and-out reprobates who become mixed up in an organ-harvesting scheme. Comedy drama, starring Nicki Micheaux, Ricardo Adam Zarate and Jon Oswald.(n)

programme.title: Bullet to the Head (2012)
programme.desc: Two hitmen are betrayed by their employer and one of them is killed, leaving the survivor swearing revenge. His search for the assassin leads him to a police detective investigating the murder of his partner, and they join forces when they realise one man is behind both deaths. Action thriller, starring Sylvester Stallone, Sung Kang and Christian Slater.(n)

programme.title: Robinson Crusoe on Mars (1964)
programme.desc: Stranded on Mars with only a monkey as a companion, an astronaut has to quickly figure out how to survive a lack of oxygen, food and water on the lifeless planet. Sci-fi drama, inspired by Daniel Defoe's classic tale, starring Paul Mantee, Adam West and Victor Lundin.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Teacher (1959)
programme.desc: A headmaster hopes to land a more prestigious job at a better school, but his pupils do not want him to leave. Comedy, starring Ted Ray, Kenneth Connor, Charles Hawtrey and Kenneth Williams.(n)

programme.title: Gentlemen Prefer Blondes (1953)
programme.desc: Lounge singers Lorelei and Dorothy embark on a transatlantic cruise to Paris in search of rich husbands. While Dorothy falls for a penniless man, Lorelei thinks she has found the millionaire of her dreams, only for his father to hire a private eye to spy on her and prove she is a gold-digger. Howard Hawks' musical comedy, starring Marilyn Monroe, Jane Russell, Charles Coburn and Elliott Reid.(n)

programme.title: The Rewrite (2014)
programme.desc: A washed-up Hollywood screenwriter takes a job at a small university, planning to exploit his position as a local celebrity and do as little work as possible. His cynical outlook begins to change when he falls for a single mother working toward her degree, while a talented pupil makes him consider what he wants from his life. Romantic comedy, with Hugh Grant, Marisa Tomei, Allison Janney and JK Simmons.(n)

programme.desc: Jeff decides to expand his social network by becoming friends with a man from his gym, but is perturbed to discover he is gay. Meanwhile, Russell learns a shocking secret about his date. Comedy, starring David Spade.(n)

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Barney puts together an extensive pre-nuptial agreement that lays out the rules of behaviour within marriage. American sitcom, starring Josh Radnor, Alyson Hannigan and Neil Patrick Harris.(n)

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Lily and Marshall struggle to find a babysitter, until her father agrees to be their nanny. Robin and Ted lock horns over who has a more serious relationship until each breaks down and has to admit the shortcomings of their significant other.(n)

programme.sub-title: Do As I Say, Not As I Did

However, she soon becomes anxious so Joe decides to follow the youngster and keep an eye on her. Elsewhere, Holly grows jealous when she and Ryder allow another girl into their study group. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: The duo are faced with a difficult dilemma when Joe comes into money, while a small leak in the ceiling grows into a huge problem for Mel. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: Molly is nervous about Mike attending a literary party that her publisher is throwing, but his feelings are hurt when he realises she doesn't want him to go. She reluctantly brings her family, but Mike is left to hang out with a human statue. Comedy, starring Billy Gardell and Melissa McCarthy.(n)

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: Mike and Vince bond while fixing a water heater, and Peggy goes back to her hometown in rural Missouri as part of Molly's research for her new book.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard faces the difficult decision of choosing between his relationship with Priya and his friendship with Penny, while Sheldon is irritated by one of Howard's magic tricks. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki and Jim Parsons.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Murray struggles to get Adam interested in hockey, but he doesn't enjoy the sport and fakes enthusiasm to please his father. Beverly tries to help Erica prepare for her college entrance exams, but her plans threaten to backfire when she does the job too well.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly replaces Murray's favourite chair with a new one without consulting him first, causing him to go on the warpath. Adam becomes tired of being picked on during the bus ride to school, but soon comes to regret enlisting Barry to help fend off the bully.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: To track down an infamous arms dealer, Peralta and Jeffords go undercover at an illegal gambling club, but when Captain Holt tries to coach them in the art of poker, his addiction resurfaces. Boyle buys a food truck that was formerly the site of a brutal double murder, to the horror of his only investor in the project, Santiago. Hitchcock and Scully have an unusual challenge for Dia. Cop comedy, starring Andy Samberg.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The Vulture makes an unwelcome return to Peralta and Santiago's lives, threatening to swoop in and take something important away from them. Meanwhile, Boyle and Diaz must track down kidnapped NYPD horse Sergeant Peanut Butter, and Holt challenges Jeffords to be less image-conscious. Cop comedy, starring Andy Samberg.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is upset when a magazine fails to mention him in an article about the paper he co-wrote with Sheldon. Meanwhile, a power cut at Mrs Wolowitz's home leads to a final family dinner in her honour. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: When Leonard and Sheldon are invited to speak at UC Berkeley, they decide to take a detour and try to meet one of their idols - Star Wars director George Lucas. Meanwhile, Raj and Amy offer to settle a dispute with a game of table tennis. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel calls on Joe for a favour when she pretends to her date, Travis, that she is an excellent cook, and is asked to put her money where her mouth is. Meanwhile, Lennox is shocked when she finds out that one of her friends has a crush on Ryder. Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Ryder turns to Joe for advice about investing in stocks and shares - but then fails to take him up on a tip, which turns out to be a profitable one. Meanwhile, Mel tries to hide her delight when Lennox and Haskell break up. Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: Mike becomes extremely vigilant about crime after his wallet is stolen from the dashboard of his squad car while he is taking a nap.(n)

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: Joyce reveals that she intends to leave the house to Victoria in her will, leaving Molly feeling shocked and disappointed.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam accidentally gets Barry kicked out of his cool group of friends, so he comes up with a plan to get his brother back in favour by convincing them he has the hacking skills to access the school's computer system to change their grades. Comedy, starring Sean Giambrone.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Pops is evicted after accidentally starting a fire in his flat during a Halloween party. Beverly invites him to move into the family home, but soon his wild lifestyle proves too much for them to handle. Comedy, starring George Segal.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and Santiago investigate a string of arson attacks that are connected to Santiago's favourite crossword puzzle author. Gina helps Holt improve his Commissioner candidate presentation, while Jeffords, Diaz, Hitchcock and Scully argue over who deserves to get a new detective car.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and Boyle realise that they have been fooled into joining a cult-like pyramid scheme and they struggle to terminate their contracts with it. Jeffords, Diaz, Gina and Holt give Santiago advice when she feels the pressure on her first day as a sergeant. US cop comedy, starring Andy Samberg and Jo Lo Truglio.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny and Leonard announce that they are moving in together, prompting Sheldon to revisit 2003, a much simpler time before he had met either of them. Howard and Raj form a band to play at the comic-book store, only to fall out over musical differences.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The boys want to become more sporty, so Barry Kripke leads them in a fencing lesson, while Penny, Amy and Bernadette help Stuart, who wants to attract more female customers to the comic-book store.(n)

programme.title: Hollyoaks Favourites
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: Adam and Andi go to great lengths to secure a loan to fix an unexpected issue on their flip house. Meanwhile, Joe enjoys hanging out with his nurse, Funches, and wants to find a way to get him to stay with him longer than planned. Comedy, starring Matt LeBlanc.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon presses Leonard and Penny to choose a date for their wedding, while he also has to deal with dramatic changes in his own relationship with Amy. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny struggles with Leonard's confession that he kissed another woman when he was away on his North Sea research expedition. After driving all the way to Las Vegas, she must now decide whether she wants to go through with the wedding. Back home, Sheldon doesn't know how to act after Amy tells him she wants them to take a relationship break.(n)

programme.title: The Heat (2013)
programme.desc: An uptight, ambitious FBI agent is sent to Boston to bring a notorious drug kingpin to justice. She ends up forced to team up with a rebellious, foul-mouthed local police detective who has a very different attitude to law enforcement but shares her inability to work with others. Action comedy, starring Sandra Bullock and Melissa McCarthy.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: In the middle of a nationwide shortage of liquid helium, Sheldon and Leonard take desperate measures to obtain the supplies they need in order to prove a scientific theory before a rival team of researchers can beat them to it. Meanwhile, Penny and Bernadette download a dating app onto Amy's phone in order to find her a new boyfriend.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: While being interviewed for a documentary about Star Trek character Mr Spock, Sheldon struggles to suppress his emotions about his recent break-up with Amy, leaving Leonard and Penny shocked to discover he had bought her an engagement ring. Howard and Bernadette fall out over plans to renovate his childhood home. Wil Wheaton, Adam Nimoy and Stephen Merchant guest star.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp enlists the help of seamstress Lisa Comfort to help Amber and Colin create a memory quilt. However, Amber's lack of ironing skills could result in disaster.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.sub-title: Canary Islands v Isle of Wight
programme.desc: Neil Smith and his partner Steph Grzyb regularly take holidays in Fuerteventura in the Canary Islands, where Neil can play golf in guaranteed sunshine. He would love to live there full-time, but Steph would rather settle on the Isle of Wight. Jasmine Harman and Sara Damergi are on hand to offer advice on the pros and cons of either move.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Pete and Kim Smith cannot decide whether to downsize in Warwickshire or buy a holiday home in the Antequera region of Spain with a view to one day moving there permanently.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Northamptonshire, where they find night-vision goggles, an antique snooker table and an enormous America army truck.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien search for more items to fix up and sell on, visiting a Norman castle, restoring a Fiat 500 and making a table from part of Southend pier.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.sub-title: Chickens, Heating and a Blank Canvas
programme.desc: Dick and Angel decide they will live as self-sufficiently as they can while restoring the chateau, and invest in some chickens to provide them with eggs for breakfast every morning. However, they still have countless problems to deal with, and have only Â£20,000 left to spend on the project. The chateau is still without either heating or water, while the 59 windows are crumbling. While Dick's renovations fall behind schedule, Angel is in her element coming up with ideas for what the finished building will look like.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Callum Hogg and Lauren Abraham's modern Park Hotel in Gloucestershire, where sisters Sue Lewis and Wendy Germann are impressed by the colourful decor, but it's not an opinion held by all. The guests are not impressed with having to pay extra for breakfast and Callum and Lauren are faced with robust feedback that leaves them wanting answers and thinking not everyone is being fair.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Trowbridge in Wiltshire to Sue's B&B, which is run by Sue Lewis and her sister, Wendy Germann. Breakfast at the communal table has its awkward moments and Sue is under pressure, having never cooked for so many before. However, it's the feedback that leaves the hosts wondering if maybe some of the guests have not been that straightforward.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to The Talbot in Iwerne Minster, Dorset for the third day of the competition, where hosts Erika Watt and Ellie Gill greet their guests. The rooms do not live up to their good first impression and Callum and Lauren are quick to find fault. At breakfast, tensions simmer as Wendy calls Cameron a creep, and at feedback, some of the comments send Erika and Ellie into fits of giggles.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The fourth visit of the week is to The Old Rectory in Llangattock, south Wales, where young hotel manager Cameron Thomas likes to keep things professional. Despite wanting to find fault, Lauren and Callum are soon to admit their bridal suite is top notch, but Erika and Ellie think there may be some cleaning issues. During an afternoon of canoe racing the group members show their competitive sides.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guest house owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. The Park Hotel in Gloucestershire is up first as owners Callum Hogg and Lauren Abraham want to get to the bottom of why Cameron didn't sleep soundly. Next, Sue Lewis from Sue's B&B seeks to discover the deasons for Callum and Lauren's low score for the cleanliness. When all issues are resolved, the winning establishment is revealed.(n)

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A couple of ex-pats attempt to run a new fishing charter, two B&B owners in France face their biggest challenge yet, and on the Costa Blanca, a family pulls together to try and rescue a Spanish dream home.(n)

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: As the competition continues, the 10 remaining potters tackle raku firing, but first the pressure is on to hand-build two animal figurines and face a tricky ceramic technique. Judges Sue Pryke and Keith Brymer Jones are on hand to decide who will be crowned Potter of the Week, and who will be leaving the competition. Hosted by Melanie Sykes.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree only have a few weeks to prepare for their wedding, but the chateau is nowhere near ready to receive the 200 guests they have invited and finding reliable, affordable workers is proving difficult. Dick oversees the damming of the moat and the installation of a new, eco-friendly sewage system, while Angel turns her attention to giving the rooms a distinctive decor.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Clinton Dall from West Sussex, a self-made businessman and father of four aiming to build one of the largest homes ever featured on the show. Dall wants the end project to be perfect - from the finish of his mirrored cruciform steel columns, to how the floor tiles line up with the dining room table, and is willing to spend whatever it takes to achieve his vision. However, the construction represents a huge risk, as down one road lies abject failure and financial ruin, while down the other, is the creation of something sublime and almost heroic.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: This time, Charlie Luxton meets Tom and Georgina in Berkshire, who are planning to renovate a run-down bungalow.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Peter Maddison meets Tabitha and Chris, who have purchased 120 acres of rainforest in Toolangi and are building a farmhouse that will be completely off grid.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow a 10-year-old who has severed his finger at school and a stonemason who has dropped a slab of marble on his hand. Meanwhile, a 74-year-old partially sighted woman is brought in by her neighbour after breaking her ankle in a fall, and draws the medical staff into stories of her challenging childhood.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts as team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson join forces with Jason Manford and Roisin Conaty respectively. Sam Simmons helps the show's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: This time, Charlie Luxton meets Tom and Georgina in Berkshire, who are planning to renovate a run-down bungalow.(n)

programme.desc: Cameras follow a 10-year-old who has severed his finger at school and a stonemason who has dropped a slab of marble on his hand. Meanwhile, a 74-year-old partially sighted woman is brought in by her neighbour after breaking her ankle in a fall, and draws the medical staff into stories of her challenging childhood.(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton travels to the mountains of southern Peru, the home of some of the world's finest coffee, before visiting a coffee maker in Europe to learn about the process that turns coffee beans into instant granules. Meanwhile, Jimmy Doherty tests high-end and cheaper versions of vodka, and Matt Tebbutt investigates Wensleydale cheese.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp looks back to the 1970s for vintage inspiration in this bite-sized episode. The presenter creates crocheted jellyfish and a colourful felt rug with the help of two of Britain's most gifted craftspeople.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Marc and Lucy Champ dream of escaping the rat race for weekends and holidays. However, the pair are torn between east Devon, where Marc grew up, and Comares in Andalucia, Spain. Will it be the weather or the properties that Laura Hamilton and Sara Damergi show them that help make up their minds?(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Lisa and Stuart Newby are looking to buy a holiday home, but are torn between locations. Australian-born Lisa, tired of the sun, craves a property on Yorkshire's dinosaur coast, however her husband and their children would prefer something near the beach in Costa Granadina, Spain. Presenters Laura Hamilton and Sara Damergi are on hand to help them decide.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole restores two quad bikes and a stylish sun lounger, while Simon O'Brien rescues a holed canoe and turns an old oak beam into a spice rack.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Essex, where Henry restores a car trailer and a 1970s racing bike, and Simon turns his attention to lovingly restoring a vintage model aircraft.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree only have a few weeks to prepare for their wedding, but the chateau is nowhere near ready to receive the 200 guests they have invited and finding reliable, affordable workers is proving difficult. Dick oversees the damming of the moat and the installation of a new, eco-friendly sewage system, while Angel turns her attention to giving the rooms a distinctive decor.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Shiralee Bed & Breakfast in Salisbury, run by Fay Lahiani and her partner Ray Cooper, where some guests are shocked by the colourful and cluttered decor, while the presence of CCTV cameras also causes concern.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At The Bluebell Woods in Northampton, David and Donna Bletsoe Brown try to bring a touch of class to their woodland B&B, but Fay Lahiani and Ray Cooper are on the warpath and are quick to discover a mucky mattress. Meanwhile, Marc Harris and Wayne Williams reveal their competitive streak during an afternoon of horse riding.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to The Lion Hotel in Pontypool for the third day of the contest, where manager Marc Harris and head chef Wayne Williams aim to deliver a high end service. Although some of the guests are impressed by the rooms, Ray and Fay find it too small for the price, and simmering tensions between some of the competitors leads to a clash over dinner. At breakfast, Wayne's food comes under fire, but it is David and Donna's feedback about finding a nail in their room that really rattles their hosts.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final guest house of the week is the New Inn, Talgarth, where ex-naval officer Damon Lewis is the sole captain of his ship. Wayne and Marc are determined to be brutally honest, and Donna and David are forced to work closely with their rivals Fay and Ray during an afternoon team-building exercise. Over dinner, Damon admits that taking on the B&B, pub and restaurant all by himself may have not been the best decision - as proved when he has trouble running a solo breakfast service.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time, with Fay and Ray from Shiralee B&B wanting to find out why David and Donna were so critical of their cleanliness. The group members continue to air their grievances as they find out what they've been paid, before the winner is revealed.(n)

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: The owners of a vineyard open a pop-up restaurant, but there are some last-minute nerves, while two B&B proprietors host an unusual event in a bid to boost takings. In Spain, a wannabe fishing captain has to learn the ropes.(n)

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: Judge Sue Pryke sets a multi-coloured slip cast challenge to make two vases, before the competitors take part in a blindfolded throwing challenge. Who will be named potter of the week, and who will leave the pottery?(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree continue their renovation of a French chateau, 18 months after the end of the first series. So far, only nine rooms have been renovated and many areas of the 12-acre estate have yet to be looked at - but the project is going to have to start paying for itself soon. They make plans to host weddings on the grounds, while looking ahead to keeping livestock and growing their own crops. Dick takes steps to generate solar power during a hot summer, and turns an old oil tank into a pig roaster.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Boat enthusiast James Strangeways invites Kevin McCloud to survey progress on the construction of his new home, which his architect nephew Ben Hebblethwaite has designed to be as 'un-houselike' as possible. James has spent the past 35 years travelling the canals and waterways of Britain, and despite never liking houses, has decided now is the time to put down roots on dry land. Ben hoped a home standing on stilts above a marsh in East Sussex, and incorporating ship-like qualities such as a keel-shaped roof, will be enough to satisfy his boat-mad uncle. However, Ben's contractor goes into liquidation a few months into the project, taking Â£87,000 of the Â£300,000 budget, and when James decides to make a few tweaks to the design, alarm-bells begin to ring for the young architect.(n)

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Part two of two. Timothy West and Prunella Scales' final journey takes them through Vietnam's Mekong Delta and across the border into Cambodia. They visit the capital city of Phenom Penh, and conclude their travels at Angkor Wat, an ancient temple built by the clever use of canals.(n)

programme.title: It Was Alright In
programme.desc: Matt Lucas narrates a look back at the decade's TV shows, meeting people who appeared in them, those who watched them and their creators. The first of two programmes focuses on pleasure and leisure on 1970s television, with featured clips including outrageous adult jokes in pre-watershed sitcoms, smoking cigarettes on prime-time panel shows, a surprising history lesson aimed at children, and St Bernard dogs being offered for prizes. The footage is also seen through the eyes of people who missed the decade altogether and who find the moral values exhibited to be a real eye-opener.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal and Ted get involved in yet another bout of rivalry with their counterparts over on Rugged Island - this time by writing a song that will allow them the dubious honour of representing Ireland in the Eurovision-style music contest Eurosong. Ardal O'Hanlon and Dermot Morgan star.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Bishop Brennan descends on Craggy Island to investigate rumours that Father Jack has developed the unfortunate habit of sleepwalking in the nude - only to come face to face with his worst fear. Clerical comedy, with Ardal O'Hanlon and Dermot Morgan.(n)

programme.title: Shocking Emergency Calls
programme.desc: A man makes a 911 call in South Carolina as he attacks his ex-girlfriend. A shocking call reveals the aftermath of an attack on a baby, and fog suddenly hits the Sheppey Crossing.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts as team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson join forces with Jason Manford and Roisin Conaty respectively. Sam Simmons helps the show's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Bishop Brennan descends on Craggy Island to investigate rumours that Father Jack has developed the unfortunate habit of sleepwalking in the nude - only to come face to face with his worst fear. Clerical comedy, with Ardal O'Hanlon and Dermot Morgan.(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton visits California to find out why there are so many closed pistachios in the packs on supermarket shelves, while Matt Tebbutt heads to Italy where he learns that UK bars or restaurants selling draught prosecco might be at risk of prosecution. Meanwhile, Jimmy Doherty heads to Ireland to investigate whether traditional rolled oats are healthier than quick-cook porridge.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp joins jewellery-maker Rachel Jeffrey to help a soon-to-be-married couple create their own wedding rings, and learns how gold can revive an old picture frame at little cost.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman and Sara Damergi help retired couple Matthew and Myra McGuigan search for a new home in either Cheltenham, Gloucestershire, or Spain's Costa del Sol. The couple have already sold their UK house and are looking to find another property quickly.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Property experts meet fitness fanatics Mike Bennett and Andree Warbrick who cannot decide between buying a property on southern Spain's Costa Tropical or opting for a bigger house in the Cotswolds.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole head to Middlesex and Surrey, where projects include repurposing a 1950s hairdryer, updating a school desk and the restoration of a child's motorbike.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel the length of Britain turning unwanted junk into profit for their owners. In this episode they're on the south coast, where Henry gets to work an aircraft fuel tank while Simon gets obsessed with an iconic '60s speedboat.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree continue their renovation of a French chateau, 18 months after the end of the first series. So far, only nine rooms have been renovated and many areas of the 12-acre estate have yet to be looked at - but the project is going to have to start paying for itself soon. They make plans to host weddings on the grounds, while looking ahead to keeping livestock and growing their own crops. Dick takes steps to generate solar power during a hot summer, and turns an old oil tank into a pig roaster.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Alston House in Cumbria, where owners Michael and Carole Allchorne are brimming with confidence, but Yvonne Copeland and Gail Wells are less than impressed with the decor. The guests are impressed with chef Michael's breakfast, but the hosts are left reeling by the criticisms levelled at their hotel during the feedback session.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Scotia House in Harrogate, where host Marie Lock is hoping her obsessive cleaning regime will give her the edge in the competition. In the rooms, both Michael and Carole and Yvonne and Gail find the deluxe teddy bears freaky, and for Billy and Billy, things are a little on the cosy side. In the afternoon, Marie's keen to show off her moves and pits her guests against each other in a Northern Soul dance off.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Canberra Guest House in Blackpool for the third day of the competition, where host Yvonne Copeland, joined by her part-time helper Gail Wells, hopes her cheap and cheerful B&B will win over her guests. However, Marie finds what she thinks is urine and hair on the toilet seat in her room, and a lack of wi-fi means Billy Junior has to put his business on hold. Yvonne takes her competitors to a local reptile house, but serious divisions unfold within the group, and later, tensions rise again at dinner.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Crown and Kitchen in East Linton, East Lothian, owned by father and son Billy and Billy Jr Robertson, who see themselves as the jokers of the pack. Yvonne and Gail leave no mattress unturned in their room inspection, although Michael and Carole are impressed. Later, Yvonne makes quite a splash when the hosts organise an assault course for their guests, but slow service during breakfast leaves the B&Bers disappointed - especially when Billy Jr is at a loss to explain what the problem was.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. Things are tense from the start as Carole and Michael from Alston House challenge Marie on her comments. Next, Marie from Scotia House wants to set the record straight with allegations of tit-for-tat scoring. The group members proceed to thrash out their differences before the week's winners are revealed.(n)

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: As the season draws to a close, a couple hoping to run a fishing charter business face a big decision. Two bed-and-breakfast owners have to cope with some tricky customers, while the first guests book into an observatory business in rural France - but how will the owners go about pleasing the punters?(n)

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: The eight remaining potters are challenged to build a pair of matching lamp bases, hoping to impress judges Sue Pryke and Keith Brymer Jones. Later, guest judge Emma Bridgewater drops in to set an emotional ceramic challenge for the contestants. Presented by Melanie Sykes.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Fresh from the success of hosting a three-day wedding at the chateau, Dick and Angel make business plans for the grounds, intending to charge people to fish in the moat and forage in the woods. Angel starts work on a second guest suite, but also wants to install an elevator, which pushes Dick's engineering skills to the limit.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie: Keep Crafting and Carry On
programme.desc: Omnibus. Kirstie Allsopp offers creative ideas and craft projects for the whole family during the coronavirus lockdown, making the most of things she already has at home. Among the things she does is make 3D flowers to represent spring and show fun makes that anyone could tackle, such as a paper garland to brighten up the home and a bug hotel to welcome insects.(n)

programme.title: Thou Shalt Not Kill
programme.desc: As Valeria and Andrea's relationship reaches breaking-point, they must find a way to work together when the charred body of Claudio Rivetti, a partner in a brick-making factory, is found in one of the firm's ovens. The body was discovered by Rami, a worker at the factory, and Valeria is suspicious of the bond between Rami and Emanuela, the wife of the murdered man. Meanwhile, Giorgio sets up Menduni. In Italian.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Mechanic Grant, 34, is rushed in after being crushed against a brick wall by his own car, which he was working on at the time. Doctors are concerned he has a serious pelvic injury and other internal wounds and work quickly to establish how severe they are. Meanwhile, 44-year-old Doug, who has a rare growth on his head and face which is prone to profuse bleeding, arrives in A&E, and 23-year-old cage fighter Charlie is treated in minor injuries for something in his eye.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A five-year-old is rushed into St George's struggling to breathe, and her mum reveals how she has spent months in Great Ormond Street Hospital after being diagnosed with a rare form of juvenile arthritis. Meanwhile, an 83-year-old man in a confused state arrives in A&E after falling over, and as doctors carry out tests and a CT scan, his wife helps them understand the reason behind his confusion. Plus, a twelve-year-old girl must be brave as she undergoes treatment for a nasty cut on her leg.(n)

programme.desc: Comedy set amongst the hustle and bustle of a low-rent, backstreet pizza parlour, following the adventures of delivery riders Joshua (Samson Kayo) and Ricky (Theo Barklem-Biggs). It's all change at Sliced, as mercurial manager Mario hires new girl Naomi to join the team. Joshua falls head over heels in love with her but things get held up when he encounters a rather challenging customer.(n)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies sets tasks to Mel Giedroyc and Noel Fielding, ultimately deciding who wins. Alex Horne helps coax the contestants.(n)

programme.title: Judge Romesh
programme.desc: A fed-up claimant appears in court to stop his friend climbing on their roof and a teacher accuses a colleague of theft in a dramatic legal showdown.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson goes on a four-day trek through the Kintail region of the north-western Highlands, examining the story of the Jacobite uprisings of the early 1700s along the way. He starts by visiting Iron Age dwellings in Shiel Bridge, and heads to the rebuilt castle on Eilean Donan, the original of which was destroyed after invading Spanish troops landed there and were attacked by British warships. Tony ends in Glen Shiel, the site of a climactic battle in 1719 where royalist troops defeated the rebels.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on a 45-mile walk across Wiltshire, from Avebury to Stonehenge, telling the story of the remarkable development that occurred in the latter days of the Neolithic era. His route over chalk downlands and Salisbury Plain takes him through one of the greatest concentrations of prehistoric sites in Europe.(n)

programme.title: Royal Murder Mysteries
programme.sub-title: The Earl of Erroll - Death in the Valley
programme.desc: Crimes involving members of royal families from around the globe, beginning with an earl shot dead in a hedonistic hideaway for colonial aristocrats in Kenya.(n)

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: The largest evacuation of the Second World War, which saw two million civilians and soldiers removed in an extraordinary German operation that lasted for four months.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The battle for North Africa, which involved three years of fighting between Rommel's Afrika Korps and Montgomery's Desert Rats and culminated in the Allied victory at El Alamein. Narrated by Laurence Olivier.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A fuel plant in Poland built to make Nazi Germany self-sufficient by using the latest technology of the time to turn coal into oil.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents an exploration of Earth's vast seas, which cover 70 per cent of the planet's surface. In the first episode, millions of squid appear off the coast of California, gannets, whale sharks and dolphins hunt migrating sardines in the Indian Ocean, and thousands of turtles make the annual pilgrimage to lay their eggs in Costa Rica.(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter continues his examination of how the car became an everyday part of people's lives, exploring the weird world of small vehicles. He discovers how austerity and fears of congestion led to European disasters in the shape of Britain's infamous three-wheelers, French death traps and German absurdities, and also takes to the battlefield to settle one of the greatest rivalries in car history.(n)

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the life and work of people in the polar regions, including the residents of Norilsk in Siberia, the coldest city on Earth. He also discovers how the Danish armed forces maintain their country's claim to Greenland's natural resources by mounting a 2,000-mile dog sled patrol during the winter, and finds out how a research base at the South Pole is able to cope with being cut off from the outside world for six months at a time.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Camera crews journey to the deepest parts of the world's oceans to explore the lives of their inhabitants, including fearsome predators with huge teeth, strange jellyfish and deep-sea sharks that lurk among the gigantic submerged mountains and volcanos. Presented by David Attenborough.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The Three Gorges Dam, built across China's Yangtze river, which is the largest and heaviest concrete structure ever built, housing the world's largest power station.(n)

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: A Scottish soldier who walked 200 miles through the Libyan desert without water or supplies recalls Ronald Moore, the leader of his party.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How the myth of German invincibility was finally debunked by their defeat at the Battle of Stalingrad. Narrated by Laurence Olivier.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A towering structure with an explosive past, a deserted cityscape, a mysterious top-secret facility and a vast Nazi construction.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering

programme.title: Porridge

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletch desperately wants to get out of the tedious task of making fishing nets, and hatches a crafty scheme to get himself assigned to the library. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker.(n)

programme.title: Blackadder

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: It was 30 years after the Romans invaded Britain that they were ready to take on the challenge of conquering the Lake District. With the toughest landscape they had encountered in the nation, peopled by a rebellious tribe, it was no small task, but within a few years, the whole of Lakeland was under their control. Tony Robinson tackles the journey by himself, walking 50 miles from Penrith to Ambleside and on to the Irish Sea at Ravenglass, discovering that the Romans encountered beauty and danger in equal measure.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson takes a four-day trek along the coastline between Plymouth in Devon and Falmouth, learning about the thriving smuggling trade in Cornwall during the late 18th century. The area's tiny secretive harbours, beaches and secluded coves were ideal for illicit imports such as tobacco and brandy, and the business was so huge it threatened the national economy. Along his route, visiting such beauty spots as Lantic Bay and Polperro, he encounters all sorts of reminders of the trade and meets descendants of those involved.(n)

programme.title: Royal Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: Calls to reinvestigate the suspicious death of King Ludwig of Bavaria in 1886, who died three days after he was declared insane and deposed from the throne.(n)

programme.title: The World at War

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The creation of a set of Second World War sea defences built near the mouth of the River Thames to protect London. Plus, a concrete pyramid build during the Cold War.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Camera crews journey to the deepest parts of the world's oceans to explore the lives of their inhabitants, including fearsome predators with huge teeth, strange jellyfish and deep-sea sharks that lurk among the gigantic submerged mountains and volcanos. Presented by David Attenborough.(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James concludes his examination of the social significance of cars by exploring how aspiration and new wealth were behind the development of some of the greatest models ever made. He tries to make sense of the baffling world of company car hierarchy by holding a travelling salesman race-off and indulges his 1980s urges with the twin delights of a Lamborghini and a Porsche. He also unveils his choice of the ultimate people's car - a vehicle that can lay claim to being the greatest in history.(n)

programme.title: Madagascar
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates the story of the island in the Indian Ocean, off the southeastern coast of Africa. It has an ecosystem that has stood in isolation for millions of years, and produced an array of wildlife that cannot be found anywhere else on the planet. He begins by observing lemurs in their habitat, male red giraffe-necked weevils fighting each other, the courting rituals of chameleons, and the behaviour of spiders and fossas.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Predators and prey in the open oceans come under the scrutiny of David Attenborough. Marlin track down a shoal of sardines as a sei whale scoops up the leftovers, dolphins herd mackerel and attract the attention of a sailfish with the resonance of their sonar, and giant sunfish queue up to allow other species, including seagulls, to feed on their parasites.(n)

programme.title: Blackadder

programme.title: Blackadder

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: It was 30 years after the Romans invaded Britain that they were ready to take on the challenge of conquering the Lake District. With the toughest landscape they had encountered in the nation, peopled by a rebellious tribe, it was no small task, but within a few years, the whole of Lakeland was under their control. Tony Robinson tackles the journey by himself, walking 50 miles from Penrith to Ambleside and on to the Irish Sea at Ravenglass, discovering that the Romans encountered beauty and danger in equal measure.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on another expedition through some of Britain's most historic landscapes. He begins with a towpath exploration of industrial engineering along the Leeds and Liverpool Canal. Setting off from Liverpool, he takes a three-day walk inland to Wigan, ending at the town's iconic pier as he traces the story of the waterway's chaotic construction and its impact on the development of Lancashire.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson explores the history of the ancient kingdom on a walk from Melrose in the Scottish Borders to Holy Island off the coast of Northumberland. Over five days, he goes on the trail of one of Northumbria's greatest sons - Saint Cuthbert. Through the Tweed Valley and across the Cheviot Hills, it's a journey through a murky history laden with myth. However, by investigating the communities, power bases and landscape of seventh-century Northumbria, Tony aims to understand the pagan land in which Cuthbert spread his simple brand of Celtic Christianity.(n)

programme.title: Royal Murder Mysteries
programme.sub-title: Albert Victor: The Prince and the Ripper
programme.desc: Sordid speculation dogged the life of Prince Albert Victor, who was even a suspect in the Jack the Ripper case.(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry.(n)

programme.title: Madagascar
programme.desc: An observation of the wildlife that inhabits the narrow band of mountains and rainforest on the island's east coast, from ring-tailed lemurs living on an inhospitable peak, to forest-dwelling sifakas, small primates on the verge of extinction. The programme also focuses on the golden bamboo lemur, which is capable of consuming daily doses of cyanide, and looks at the fruit-rich treetops where red-ruffed lemurs defend their food and territory. Narrated by David Attenborough.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Marine life at the poles, where winter temperatures frequently fall to a numbing -40C. Even when the sun reappears in the spring, its weak rays take a long time to melt the ice. David Attenborough describes how wildlife including seals, whales, polar bears and penguins overcome the elements to survive through prolonged cold spells.(n)

programme.title: The World at War

programme.title: Location, Location, Location

They begin in the popular commuter county of Kent, where homes are snapped up quickly due to their proximity to London. Robin and Gemma have recently returned to the UK after three years in Singapore and want a family home to bring up their baby, while in Tunbridge Wells, Gemma and Barney are looking to buy their first place together in a very competitive market.(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray gets the chance to cover the Super Bowl for his newspaper - but feels guilty about not asking Debra to accompany him. Ray Romano stars.(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is horrified to learn that his mother used to read the diary he kept as a teenager, which contained his most personal thoughts. Ray Romano stars.(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Feuding brothers Niles and Frasier decide to find out once and for all which of them has the higher IQ. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce.(n)

programme.desc: The Seattle shrink takes a dislike to KACL's newest presenter, sharp-tongued radio psychiatrist Dr Nora. Award-winning episode of the comedy series, guest starring Christine Baranski and Piper Laurie.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Engaged duo Tony and Ami kick off the competition in East Sussex with an evening of raucous fun, but foodies Chris and Charlene are baffled by the cultural references, and Polish pair Izabella and Michal are left tired from chewing on Ami's rock-solid banoffee pies. programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Comedian James Acaster, actor Russell Tovey, TV presenter Rylan Clark-Neal and actress Michelle Keegan take on three challenges as the contest continues. Presented by Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding.(n)

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series is centred around the roads of Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong.(n)

programme.title: Nothing to Declare

programme.title: Nothing to Declare

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series focuses on Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong.(n)

programme.title: Brits Behind Bars
programme.desc: With thousands of UK citizens currently held in jails around the world, this documentary focuses on the moving cases of British prisoners. Those who break the law overseas face potentially lethal consequences - nightmare 'living hell' conditions, disease, abuse, terror, and sometimes even death. Meeting families and lawyers forging major battles to get fair treatment for their loved ones, the programme reveals trials that have gone catastrophically wrong, dubious evidence, and one man who has been struggling to prove his innocence for almost three decades.(n)

programme.desc: Prince Charles pays a personal tribute to Her Majesty to mark her 60-year reign. He reflects on various public events and private family moments over the past six decades and shows previously unseen photographs and home movies - some of which were shot by the Queen herself.(n)

programme.title: The October Man (1947)
programme.desc: A chemist with suicidal tendencies is falsely accused of murder - and his precarious mental state is further upset by taunts from the real killer. Psychological thriller, adapted from a novel by Eric Ambler, starring John Mills, Joan Greenwood and Joyce Carey.(n)

programme.desc: Fiona Bruce explores the stories behind official royal residences, which act as family homes and places to conduct the business of monarchy, and charts the evolution of their architecture. She begins with Buckingham Palace and learns about its links to London sewers, its features including state rooms and a home for camels and elephants, plus how its chandeliers are cleaned.(n)

programme.desc: The team are soon on the scene after a journalist is slashed and stabbed to death in broad daylight. Although no witnesses are willing to come forward, the evidence suggests it was a gangland killing connected to local racial tensions - and as the MIT pursue their investigations, they find themselves plunged into a community tragically torn by hatred and deceit. Starring Lindsey Coulson and Samantha Spiro.(n)

programme.desc: The Government threatens to trim the Lords' powers if they rebel again and Lord Strathclyde publishes his review into the powers the upper house have to block legislation.
